title: आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule
content: आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule
intro: If masculine-singular ends with आ(A), forms follow: आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई for m.s.-f.s.-n.s.-m.p.-f.p.-n.p.

blocks.heading: Rule
blocks.content: Endings: masculine singular आ (A), feminine singular ई (I), neuter singular ए (e), masculine plural ए (e), feminine plural या (yA), neuter plural ई (I).

blocks.heading: Note 1: स(s) -> श(sh)
blocks.content: When ending with consonant स(s), use श(sh) instead for feminine singular, feminine plural, neuter plural.

blocks.heading: Note 2: च, ज, झ
blocks.content: Heavier sound for feminine singular, feminine plural, neuter plural.

blocks.heading: Note 2: Words with च, ज, झ

title: जाना चाहिये करना चहिये jAnA chAhiye karanA chahiye etc Should in Marathi
content:
In Hindi to indicate the desired behaviour, we say उसने पढाई करनी चाहिये (usane paDhAI karanI chAhiye),सबने फल खाने चाहिये (sabane phal khAne chAhiye ) etc.
In Marathi, sentence formation is:-
Form of कर्ता noun/pronoun
as in simple past tense
verb+ “आयला “ (AyalA)
पाहिजे  (pAhije)/ form of हवे (have)
as per gender and multiplicity of noun
form ofअसणे(“asaNe”)
as per tense
त्याने
जायला
पाहिजे/ हवे
आहे
Form of हवे (have) are हवा-हवी-हवे-हवे-हव्या-हवी (havA-havI-have-have-havyA-havI) this follows
आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule
उसने आम खाना चाहिये था (usane Am khAnA chAhiye thA)
त्याने आंबा खायला पाहिजे/हवा होता (tyAne AMbA khAyalA pAhije/havA hotA)
उसने इमली खानी चाहिये थी (usane imalI khAnI chAhiye thI)
त्याने चिंच खायला पाहिजे/हवी होती (tyAne chiMch khAyalA pAhije/havI hotI)

title: नाम लिंग वचन Nouns Gender and plurals
content:
Interestingly there is no specific rules based on which gender is decided !! So you have to do your guess. As you keep reading, listening Marathi more and more you will now about what is considered what.
Similarly depending on the gender and ending vowel of the word it plural is formed differently.
I have tried to summarize few rules here
Gender
Ending vowel
Plural form will
end with
Singular –Plural Example
Meaning in English
Masculine
आ (A)
ए (e)
फळा  - फळे
phaLA – phaLe
फुगा – फुगे
phugA – phuge
Board - Boards
Ballon -Ballons
Feminine
ई (I)
या (yA )
बरणी - बरण्या
baraNI baraNyA
सावली - सावल्या
sAvalI - sAvalyA
jar-jars
shadow - shadows
Feminine
अ(a)
आ(A)
ई  (I)
माळ – माळा
mAL - mALA
मांजर - मांजरी
mAMjar - mAMjarI
garland - garlands
cat - cats
Neuter
अ(a)
ए (e)
पान – पाने
pAn - pAne
leaf - leaves
Neuter
ए (e)
ई  (I)
पाते - पाती
pAte - pAtI
blade - blades
Neuter
ऊ (U)
ए (e)
पिल्लू पिल्ले
pillU - pille
chick - chicken
Note for Feminine ending with अ(a) there are two ways of creating plural. For some plural ends using आ(A) and for some plural ends with ई (I). There is no specific rule which one to use when. You will understand it as you will read more and more Marathi.
For other words that do not fit in the above rules, the singular and plural forms are same.
e.g. earthen pot is called माठ in Marathi which is treated as masculine. So its plural form is माठ only
e.g. 1 earthen pot 10 earthen pots 1 माठ – 10 माठ
I have started creating list of Marathi nouns, their gender and plural forms. List is uploaded as an excel sheet at
https://sites.google.com/site/learnmarathionline/home/nouns-gender-plural-and-forms-for-prepositions
Download the excel sheet to view it correctly.
Excel sheet is not up-to-date. I will keep adding more and more nouns. So please check this link frequently to see newly added nouns.

title: मेरे पास है. मुझे दो भाई है. mere pAs hai.mujhe do bhAI hai. To Indicate possession and relation
content:
In Hindi, we say मेरे पास घर है(mere pAs ghar hai), मेरे पास गाडी है(mere pAs gADI hai). Such sentences are said in Marathi, the exact same way.
In Marathi such sentences are formed as follows:
सामन्यरूप(sAmanyarUp) form
of my/his/ ‘s etc.
जवळ(javaL)/कडे(kaDe )
noun
form of “to be” i.e.असणे (asaNe) as per
tense and gender, multiplicity of noun
माझ्या (mAjhyA)
जवळ(javaL)/कडे(kaDe )
गाडी (gADI)
आहे (Ahe)

title: लागणे(lAgaNe) - one verb, multiple meanings !!
content: लागणे(lAgaNe) – one verb, multiple meanings
intro: लागणे has many meanings. Structure: subject+ला + object + लागते/लागले. Meanings: need, hurt, hungry, thirsty, program on TV, appliance on, nausea in bus.

blocks.heading: 1) To need
rows: My car needs a lot of petrol

blocks.heading: 2) Must/Have to
blocks.content: See lesson Using Must/to be have to.

blocks.heading: 5) Program/song on TV/Radio
rows: बिग बॉस मालिका नऊ वाजता लागते
big b~os mAlikA naU vAjatA lAgate

title: लावणे(lAvaNe) - one verb, multiple meanings !!
content: लावणे(lAvaNe) – one verb, multiple meanings
intro: लावणे is very commonly used. One verb, many meanings based on context. Structure: noun + लावणे. To make someone do: verb+आयला + लावणे.

blocks.heading: Touch / Park / Blame / Call / Time / Price
Wear / Cook / Sow / Tune / Color / Plant
Note
blocks.content: लावणे is causative of लागणे. She took lot of time: तीने खूप वेळ लावला. It took her lot of time: तीला खूप वेळ लागला.

title: Abuse-cursing-swear-words in Marathi मराठी गालिया मराठी शिव्या
content:
Like in any other language Marathi also has many ways to abuse or curse or using swear words. In Hindi, it is called गाली(gAlI). Its plural is गालियां (gAliyAM)
In Marathi such word is called शिवी(shivI). Its plural is शिव्या (shivyA).
To curse/ To abuse is called as शिवी/शिव्या देणे (shivI/shivyA deNe) or
शिविगाळ करणे (shivigAL karaNe)
The intention of this lesson is not to teach you Marathi शिव्या (shivyA) but to introduce you to them so that you can be aware not to use them and get alert if someone uses it.
A native Indian will already know this because most of these words are common in Marathi, Hindi and other north Indian dialects.
So this lesson is intended more for non-Indians.
Generally, swear-words are related to private organs and unethical sexual relations.
So slang words or taboo words about these as follows:
Penis :- लंड (laMD), लौडा (lauDA), लवडा (lavaDA), बुल्ला ( bullA ) ,आंड(AMD)
Testicle :- गोटी ( goTI ). Its plural is गोट्या(goTyA)
Female sex organ :- पुच्ची (puchchI) , भोसडा ( bhosaDA ), चूत (chUt), फोदरी ( phodarI ), फोदी ( phodI ) , भोक ( bhok )
Anus :- गांड ( gAMD ) , बोचा(bochA)
Pubic hair :- झाट ( jhAT ).
( Do note, this word is different from word झाड(jhAD) which means tree )
To Fuck :- झवणे (jhavaNe) , चोदणे ( chodaNe ) ,घालणे ( ghAlaNe ) टाकणे (TAkaNe)
(Do note :- The literal meaning of verbs घालणे ( ghAlaNe ) टाकणे (TAkaNe) is “To Put”. चढणे(chaDhaNe) means “To climb up”. These are not taboo verbs. They are very commonly used verbs. But while cursing these are used with double-meaning)
To Excrete:- हगणे ( hagaNe )
To urinate:- मुतणे ( mutaNe )
To get an erection in penis:- उठणे ( uThaNe )
(Note:- Literal meaning of उठणे ( uThaNe ) is “To get up”. This not taboo verb. But while cursing this used with double-meaning )
Prostitute :- रांड ( rAMD ), वेश्या ( veshyA ) , छिनाल (ChinAl )
Pimp:- भडवा ( bhaDavA ) भडव्या (bhaDavyA )
Asshole :- गांडू ( gAMDU ), लवडू(lavaDU), झाटू (jhATU)
Fool :- चुतिया (chutiyA), चुतिये (chutiye), चुत्त्या (chuttyA)
Motherfucker!!:- मादरचोद (mAdarachod), आईझवाड्या (AIjhavADyA), आयझवाड्या (AyajhavADyA), आईघाल्या, (AIghAlyA), आयघाल्या(AyaghAlyA)
SisterFucker !! :- भेन्चोद(bhenchod)
Fucker !!:- भिकारचोट(bhikArachoT), चुतमारिच्या(chutamArichyA), झवाड्या(jhavADyA)
Son of a bitch !!:- रांडेच्या (rAMDechyA), फोकनीच्या (phokanIchyA),फोदरीच्या (phodarIchyA)
Your mother’s ass:- तुझ्या आईची गांड (tujhyA AIchI gAMD),
Fuck your mother:- तुझी आई झवली tujhI AI jhavalI ), आई घातली ( AI ghAtalI )
Suck my dick!!:- तोंडात घे (toMDAt ghe ), चोख माझा लवडा ( chokh mAjhA lavaDA)
Is your dick hard!!:- उठला का तुझा ( uThalA kA tujhA )
To fuck one’s ass:- गांड मारणे ( gAMD mAraNe )
These are the most commonly used swear-word. There are much more special to the particular dialect of Marathi. There are words based on the social background of the person of imagination and creativity of speaker 🙂

title: Abuse Cursing Swear-words in Marathi
content: Abuse Cursing Swear-words in Marathi
intro: In Marathi swear words are called शिवी (shivI). To curse/abuse: शिवी देणे or शिविगाळ करणे. This lesson is for awareness only – not to teach use.

blocks.heading: Purpose
blocks.content: For non-Indians to be aware and avoid. Most terms are common across Marathi, Hindi, and north Indian dialects.

title: Active Passive voice कर्तरी प्रयोग कर्मणी प्रयोग भावे प्रयोग kartarI prayog karmaNI prayog bhAve prayog
content:
Please refer lesson from my “Learn Marathi from English” blog for grammar details of
Active Passive voice i.e. कर्तरी प्रयोग(kartarI prayog), कर्मणी प्रयोग(karmaNI prayog) and भावे प्रयोग (bhAve prayog ) in Marathi
Active Passive voice in Marathi

title: Active Passive voice in Marathi - Style2
content:
In the previous lesson, we learned how to say sentences in the passive voice in Marathi
(Refer:-
Active Passive voice in Marathi
)
There is another way to say sentences in passive voice using verb येणे (yeNe)
The sentence structure is almost similar to the previous style with minor changes.
We do not use the simple past tense form of the main verb. Instead, we use
verb + ण्यात(NyAt)
We use the form of verb येणे (yeNe) instead of जाणे (jANe). This form is created the same as we have seen in the last lesson
sentence structure is:-
form of कर्ता(kartA) with preposition
कडून(kaDUn)
कर्म (karm) i.e. noun
verb + ण्यात(NyAt)
Form of verb येणे (yeNe) as per given tense
and gender ,multiplicity ofकर्म (karm) i.e. noun
माझ्याकडून (mAjhyAkaDUn)
आंबा (AMbA)
खाण्यात (khANyAt)
येतो (yeto)
Below are the examples:-
The sequence is as follows active voice sentence, passive voice sentence in the previous style, passive voice sentence in style of this lesson.
I have first given example in the previous style and then
1) He plays cricket -> तो क्रिकेट खेळतो ( to krikeT kheLato )
Cricket is played by him ->
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळले जाते ( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLale jAte )
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळण्यात येते ( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLaNyAt yete )
( Note :- क्रिकेट( krikeT ) is a neuter-singular word in Marathi. )
2) He will play cricket -> तो क्रिकेट खेळेल( to krikeT kheLel)
Cricket will be played by him ->
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळले जाईल ( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLale jAIl )
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळण्यात येईल ( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLaNyAt yeIl )
3) He will be playing cricket -> तो क्रिकेट खेळत असेल ( to krikeT kheLat asel )
Cricket will be being played by him ->
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळले जात असेल ( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLale jAt asel )
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळण्यात येत असेल ( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLaNyAt yet asel )
4) He played cricket ->
Note such sentence is by default in passive voice in Marathi
But by literally following above rule passive
Cricket was played by him ->
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळले गेले( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLale gele )
त्याच्याकडून क्रिकेट खेळण्यात आले( tyAchyAkaDUn krikeT kheLaNyAt Ale )
Ram rides a bicycle -> राम सायकल चालवतो (rAm sAyakal chAlavato )
The cycle is ridden by Ram ->
रामकडून सायकल चालवली जाते ( rAmakaDUn sAyakal chAlavalI jAte )
रामकडून सायकल चालवण्यात येते ( rAmakaDUn sAyakal chAlavaNyAt yete )
Ram will ride cycle -> राम सायकल चालवेल (rAm sAyakal chAlavel)
The cycle will be ridden by Ram ->
रामकडून सायकल चालवली जाईल ( rAmakaDUn sAyakal chAlavalI jAIl )
रामकडून सायकल चालवण्यात येईल ( rAmakaDUn sAyakal chAlavaNyAt yeIl )
Ram will be riding cycle ->राम सायकल चालवत असेल (rAm sAyakal chAlavat asel )
The cycle will be being ridden by Ram ->
रामकडून सायकल चालवली जात असेल (rAmakaDUn sAyakal chAlavalI jAt asel )
रामकडून सायकल चालवण्यात येत असेल (rAmakaDUn sAyakal chAlavaNyAt yet asel

title: Active Passive voice in Marathi
content: Active Passive voice in Marathi
intro: Three प्रयोग: कर्तरी (active), कर्मणी (passive – verb by object), भावे (passive – verb neuter). कर्मणी: कर्ता+कडून, कर्म, verb past+जा.

blocks.heading: Three types of प्रयोग
blocks.content: कर्तरी प्रयोग = active (verb by subject). कर्मणी प्रयोग = passive (verb by object). भावे प्रयोग = verb does not change by subject or object.

blocks.heading: Prepositions with passive
blocks.content: While being given: दिले जाताना. For being opened: उघडले जाण्यासाठी. After being killed: मारला गेल्यानंतर.

title: Add a question tag
content: Add a question tag
intro: In Marathi, add ना (nA) to the sentence and pronounce with question tone. Same for all tenses and subjects.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: She did not speak, did she?

title: Adjectives in Marathi मराठी विशेषण
content:
Adjectives in Marathi behave the same way as in Hindi. i.e. form of adjective word change as per gender and multiplicity of the noun.
e.g. In Hindi, we say काला घोडा ( kAlA ghoDA), काली घोडी ( kAlI ghoDI ), काले घोडे(kAle ghoDe)
We used form काला for पुल्लिंग-एकवचन, काली for स्त्रीलिंग-एकवचन and काले for पुल्लिंग-बहुवचन
Consider other adjective सफेद(saphed). We will say सफेद घोडा ( saphed ghoDA), सफेद घोडी ( saphed ghoDI ), सफेद घोडे(saphed ghoDe)
Here we used “सफेद” for all genders and multiplicity.
So the change of form is only applicable to adjective ending with आ(A).
This is exactly same in Marathi.

title: Adjectives in Marathi
content: Adjectives in Marathi
intro: Like in English we have adjectives in Marathi, to describe more about noun/pronoun. In Marathi usage of adjectives can change according to the gender and multiplicity of the noun. Generally, masculine adjectives ending with आ(A) change as per gender and multiplicity.

blocks.heading: Rule 1: Adjectives ending with आ(A)
blocks.content: If masculine adjective ends with आ(A) then feminine ends with ई(I) and neuter ends with ए(e). In Marathi: Horse (घोडा) is masculine, chair (खुर्ची) is feminine, page (पान) is neuter. Same rules for plurals apply to both adjective and noun.

blocks.heading: Rule 2: Other adjectives
blocks.content: Other adjectives generally remain the same in all genders. e.g. long: लांब (lAMb), wide: रुंद (ruMd)

blocks.heading: Rule 3: सामान्य रूप (sAmAnya rUp) with preposition
blocks.content: When adjective ending with आ(A) is used with preposition, its form changes same as noun. सामान्य रूप created by appending या (yA).

blocks.heading: Rule 4: Other adjectives with preposition
blocks.content: Other adjectives generally remain the same even when used with the preposition. Only the noun changes.

blocks.heading: Rule 5: Verb as adjective
blocks.content: In English we use 'Speaking Doll', 'Talking book' etc. In Marathi: append णारा(NArA)/णारी(NArI)/णारे(NAre) to verb for masculine/feminine/neuter. For past tense: append लेला(lelA)/लेली(lelI)/लेले(lele).

title: Adjectives made from verb
content: Adjectives made from verb
intro: Three types: (1) णारा for general behaviour (speaking, laughing); (2) लेला for completed action; (3) verb+त + असलेला for ongoing.

blocks.heading: 1) णारा – general behaviour
blocks.content: verb + णारा. Follows आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule.

blocks.heading: 2) लेला – completed action
blocks.content: verb + लेला. Follows आ-ई-ए rule.

blocks.heading: 3) Ongoing – verb+त + असलेला
blocks.content: For 'who is doing' at moment.

title: Adjectives related to prepositions
content: Adjectives related to prepositions
intro: Add चा(chA) or ला(lA) to preposition. Use सामान्य रूप of noun. वरचा, आतला, बाहेरचा etc.

title: Adverbs in Marathi मराठी क्रियाविशेषण marAThI kriyAvisheShaN
content:
In Marathi, adverbs are created in different ways.
Below I have discussed different ways in which adverbs are formed, but there is no specific rule which dictates which way to use when.
It is just custom that dictates the creation of adverbs
So as you will read more and more Marathi you will understand different adverbs.

title: Adverbs in Marathi - Part 1
content: Adverbs in Marathi - Part 1
intro: In Marathi adverbs are formed in different ways. Noun ending पणा + ए = adverb. सामान्यरूप + ने = adverb. Adverbs come before verb.

blocks.heading: पणा + ए
blocks.content: Noun ending पणा + ए. Quietly: शांतपणा + ए = शांतपणे. Sordidly: हलकटपणा + ए = हलकटपणे. Correctly: अचूकपणे. Easily: सहजपणे/सहजतेने. Independently: स्वतंत्रपणे.

blocks.heading: सामान्यरूप + ने
blocks.content: शांतपणाने, सबूरीने (patiently), उत्सुकतेने (eagerly), सहजतेने (easily), मोठ्याने (loudly).

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: ती हे काम सहजपणे संपवू शकते
tI he kAm sahajapaNe saMpavU shakate

title: Asking address in Marathi
content: Asking address in Marathi
intro: Frequently used words while describing an address.

title: Asking Question in Marathi.मराठी प्रश्न
content: There are different ways of asking the question in Hindi, and the same ways are followed in Marathi as well.

title: Basic questions and "WH" questions in Marathi
content:
Consider sentences :
Do you go?
Did he go?
Will she speak?
Was he dancing?
These are all basic questions in English. In Marathi basic questions can be formed by adding का (kA). i.e. by appending का (kA) to a statement it will be converted to question.
He goes -> Does he go
तो जातो (to jAto) ->
तो जातो का?
( to jAto kA ?)
She went -> did she go?
ती गेली (tI gelI ) ->
ती गेली का ?
(tI gelI kA)
They will go -> will they go?
ते जातील (te jAtIl) ->
ते जातील का ?
(te jAtIl kA)
Such questions can also be asked by using the same sentence but just changing the tone. In English as well we do it some times.
e.g You are coming -> You coming ??
तू येतो आहेस
(tU yeto Ahes )
You told him secret –> You told him secret ??
तू त्याला रहस्य सांगितलेस
(tU tyAlA rahasy sAMgitales )

title: bolane do karane do बोलने दो करने दो type sentences
content:
In Hindi sentences like “Let him play”, “Let me say” are said as उसे खेलने दो ( use khelane do )मुझे जाने दो ( mujhe jAne do )
In Marathi such sentence is formed by appending “ऊ दे”( U de) to the verb.
This is an imperative/command statement. So when saying someone with respect you will append “ऊ द्या” (U dyA).
Examples will make it clear
Examples :
उसे खेलने दे ( use khelane de )
त्याला खेळू दे ( tyAlA kheLU de )
उसे खेलने दो/दीजिये ( use khelane do/dIjiye )
त्याला खेळू द्या ( tyAlA kheLU dyA )
मुझे खाने दे ( mujhe khAne de
मला खाउ दे ( malA khAu de )
मुझे खाने दो/दीजिये ( mujhe khAne do/dIjiye )
मला खाउ द्या ( malA khAu dyA )
उसे ( स्त्री.) बोलने दे ( use ( strI.) bolane de )
तीला बोलू दे ( tIlA boLU de )
उसे ( स्त्री.) बोलने दो/दीजिये ( use ( strI.) bolane do/dIjiye )
तीला बोलू द्या ( tIlA boLU dyA )
तुम सब उसे गाने दो ( tum sab use gAne do )
तुम्ही सगळे त्याला गाऊ द्या ( tumhI sagaLe tyAlA gAU dyA )

title: Calling or Addressing someone in Marathi
content: Calling or Addressing someone in Marathi
intro: अरे/अगं for casual; अहो for respect. अरे for boy, अगं for girl. Do not call a girl अगं मैत्रिणी.

title: Calling someone in Marathi मराठीमें किसीको बुलाना marAThImeM kisIko bulAnA
content:
Suppose you want help and you want to get the attention of a person in front of you. In English, you may say “Hello!”, “Hey”, “Friend” etc. In Hindi, we say “अरे भाई (are bhAI), अरे भैया(are bhaiyA) etc.
These words “Hello!”, “Hey” etc. is what I mean by the title “Calling or Addressing” someone.
The way you call someone who is of your age or you are friendly with will differ from calling an elder.
In Marathi “अरे”/”अगं” (are/agaM) is used for casual or friendly address. “अहो” (aho) is used for calling with respect.
“अरे”(are) is for calling boy. “अगं” (agaM) is for calling girl.

title: Cases in Marathi: विभक्ति Vibhakti and विभक्तिप्रत्यय vibhaktiprtatyay in Marathi
content: Cases in Marathi. विभक्ति Vibhakti and विभक्तिप्रत्यय vibhaktipratyay in Marathi
intro: विभक्ति (vibhakti) and विभक्तिप्रत्यय (vibhaktipratyay) are complex. This blog teaches Marathi via prepositions instead.

blocks.heading: Why prepositions instead
blocks.content:
विभक्तिप्रत्यय (vibhaktipratyay) table includes many suffixes not used in contemporary language. In modern languages, cases were replaced by prepositions. Sanskrit/Marathi: 7 cases; German: 4; English: 2.

This blog teaches Marathi in terms of English prepositions:
- Second विभक्ति (vibhakti) = preposition "To"
- Third विभक्ति = preposition "by" (as in past tense)

Refer lessons: Preposition TO, Working with nouns – Prepositions.

title: chaleM jAyeM kareM चलें जायें करें type Questions
content:
Suppose we want to call our friends for some action, we will say
“चलो क्रिकेट खेलें”( chalo krikeT kheleM ), “चलो, सिनेमा देखें” ( chalo, sinemA dekheM), “आज गणित पढें क्या ?” ( Aj gaNit paDheM kyA ? ) etc.
These sentences are said in Marathi as
follows:
–
चलो क्रिकेट खेलें ( chalo krikeT kheleM )
चला क्रिकेट खेळूया ( chalA krikeT kheLUyA )
चलो, सिनेमा देखें ( chalo, sinemA dekheM )
चला सिनेमा बघूया ( chalA sinemA baghUyA )
चलो घर जाते हैं ( chalo ghar jAte haiM )
चला घरी जाऊया ( chalA gharI jAUyA )
खाना खाते हैं ( khAnA khAte haiM )
जेवूया ( jevUyA )

title: Combining consonants in Marathi मराठी जोडाक्षरे
content: Combining consonants in Marathi मराठी जोडाक्षरे
intro: Learn how two or more consonants combine in Marathi. Half symbol + full symbol = जोडाक्षर (joDAkShar).

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
In previous chapters, we have learned pronunciation of the Marathi alphabet. In this lesson let's learn combining consonants in Marathi.

Like in any language, two or more consonants can be combined and pronounced together in Marathi.

**Examples:**
- In the English word "fact" consonants "c" and "t" are pronounced together
- In the English word "Alp" consonants "l" and "p" are pronounced together
- In the English word "plethora" consonants "p" and "l" are pronounced together

In English, we just write consonants one after another to indicate their combination. But in Marathi, we do not just write symbols one after another. There is a little change done in the symbol of the first consonant which is to be pronounced half. So the consonant symbol is also written half. So it is important to learn Marathi writing system for such combined sounds.

Half symbol(s) + full symbol is called **जोडाक्षर** (joDAkShar)

For each symbol its corresponding "half" is predefined. I have grouped consonants depending on their logic to create "half" symbol.

blocks.heading: Group 1: Symbols with vertical line
blocks.content: Their half-symbol is created by omitting the vertical line.

blocks.heading: Group 1 – Full symbol, Half symbol, Example with व (v)
Group 2: Slanting line below consonant
blocks.content: Half sound is denoted by giving slanting line below consonant.

blocks.heading: Group 2 – Full symbol, Half symbol, Example with व (v)
Group 3: Symbol ळ (L)
blocks.content:
The small vertical line in ळ is removed to make half-symbol: ळ्‌

**Examples:** ळ्‌क, ळ्‌ब, ळ्‌व

blocks.heading: Group 4: Symbol क (k)
blocks.content:
The right side curve is written half and connected together with the next symbol: क्‌

**Examples:** क्व, क्प, क्ल, क्न

blocks.heading: Group 5: Irregular – र (r) and ह (h)
blocks.content: र (r) and ह (h) have irregular or multiple half-symbols depending on the other consonant. We will learn them separately in the next chapters.

title: Combining consonants in Marathi मराठी जोडाक्षरे
content:
In previous chapters, we have learned pronunciation of the Marathi alphabet. In this lesson let’s learn combining consonants in Marathi.
Like in any language, two or more consonants can be combined and pronounced together in Marathi.
e.g. In the English word “fact” consonants “c” and “t” and pronounced together
In the English word “Alp” consonants “l” and “p” and pronounced together
In the English word “plethora” consonants “p” and “l” and pronounced together
In English, we just write consonants one after another and by to indicate their combination. But in Marathi, we do not just write symbols one after another. There is little change done in the symbol of the first consonant which is to be pronounced half. So the consonant symbol is also written half. So it is important to learn Marathi writing system for such combined sounds.
Half symbol(s) + full symbol is called जोडाक्षर (joDAkShar)
For each symbol it’s corresponding “half” is predefined. I have grouped consonants depending on their logic to create “half” symbol

title: Combining consonants with र(r)-मराठी जोडाक्षरे Part 2
content: Combining consonants with र(r)-मराठी जोडाक्षरे Part 2
intro: This chapter focuses on combining consonants with र (r). When र is first (half) or second (full), different symbols are used.

blocks.heading: Style 1: Curve above consonant
blocks.content: Consider English words "part", "harp", "mark" etc. In all these words sound of "r" is half. So transliterating these words in Marathi they will be written by giving curve above consonant which comes after "r".

blocks.heading: Style 2: Curve before consonant
blocks.content:
This is used only where the sound of र (r) is a bit light. There is no stress on r.

सुऱ्या (suryA), दऱ्या (daryA), हऱ्या (haryA)

**By changing style 1 to style 2 you indicate different meaning:**
- सुऱ्याची (surxyAchI) means "of knife". And सुर्याची means "of the Sun"
- दऱ्या (darxyA) means "valleys". And दर्या (daryA) means "sea"

blocks.heading: When र (r) is second consonant
blocks.content: When र (r) is pronounced full but the previous consonant is pronounced half:

blocks.heading: Rule 1: Small slanting line below consonant
blocks.content:
e.g. In English word Prophet, "P" is pronounced half and "r" is full.

**Examples:**
- Prophet: प्रोफेट (propheT)
- Cockroach: कॉक्रोच (k~okroch)
- भ्रमर (bhramar) – black beetle
- ग्रहण (grahaN) – Eclipse
- तीव्र (tIvr) – intense
- सहस्र (sahasr) – thousand

blocks.heading: Rule 2: Arrow-like symbol for ट, ठ, ड, ढ, छ
blocks.content:
Used with ट (T), ठ (Th), ड (D), ढ (Dh), छ (Ch). An arrow-like symbol is given below the consonant.

**Examples:**
- Tram: ट्राम (TrAm)
- Drum: ड्रम (Dram)
- Driver: ड्रायव्हर (DrAyavhar)
- ठ्र (Thr), ढ्र (Dhr), छ्र (Chr)

blocks.heading: Rule 3: Special symbols for त and श
blocks.content:
त + र = त्र (tr)
श + र = श्र (shr)

blocks.heading: Rule 4: Curve below for ऋ (RU)
blocks.content:
When ऋ (RU) is added to any consonant it is written by giving curve below consonant. (Note: The pronunciation of ऋ is similar to रु (ru) or रू (rU).)

**Examples:** सृ (sru), कृ (kru), तृ (tru)
- सृजन (srujan) – creation
- कृष्ण (kruShN) – Name of an Indian deity
- तृण (truN) – grass

title: Combining consonants with ह(h)-मराठी जोडाक्षरे Part 3
content: Combining consonants with ह(h)-मराठी जोडाक्षरे Part 3
intro: ह (h) is a consonant that is not generally added with other consonants. There are very few words in which ह (h) is the first consonant and so pronounced half.

blocks.heading: Standard and traditional ways
blocks.content:
We can denote half sound of ह (h) by the standard way of giving slanting line below ह (h) i.e. ह्‌

But there are few peculiar ways in which such combined sound is written traditionally:

title: Combining sentences - change in form of verb असणे(asaNe)
content: Combining sentences – change in form of verb असणे(asaNe)
intro: Combine sentences by changing असणे form: आहे→असला तरी, बघितला→बघितल्यामुळे. Use सामान्यरूप असल्या + मुळे/ने.

blocks.heading: Example – combining with तरी and मुळे
rows: This is old video. I saw it. I am sharing today
हा जुना व्हिडिओ आहे. मी आत्ता बघितला. मी आज शेअर करत आहे
hA junA vhiDio Ahe. mI AttA baghitalA. mI Aj shear karat Ahe
This is old video, yet I saw it so I am sharing today
हा जुना व्हिडिओ असला तरी मी आत्ता बघितल्यामुळे आज शेअर करत आहे
hA junA vhiDio asalA tarI mI AttA baghitalyAmuLe Aj shear karat Ahe

blocks.heading: Example 1 – Present tense
rows: This is old song. I heard it today
हे जुने गाणे आहे. मी आज ऐकले
he june gANe Ahe. mI Aj aikale
Though it is old song, I heard it today
हे जुने गाणे असले तरी मी आज ऐकले
he june gANe asale tarI mI Aj aikale

blocks.heading: Example 2 – Past tense
rows: My wife was standing there. He asked about girlfriend
माझी बायको तिथे उभी होती. त्याने माझ्या मैत्रिणीबद्दल विचारले
mAjhI bAyako tithe ubhI hotI. tyAne mAjhyA maitriNIbaddal vichArale
My wife was standing there yet he asked
माझी बायको तिथे उभी असली तरी त्याने माझ्या मैत्रिणीबद्दल विचारले
mAjhI bAyako tithe ubhI asalI tarI tyAne mAjhyA maitriNIbaddal vichArale

blocks.heading: Example 3 – असल्यामुळे / असल्याने
rows: Policeman was watching. He did not dare to run
पोलिस त्याला बारकाईने बघत होता. त्याने पळून जायचे धाडस केले नाही
polis tyAlA bArakAIne baghat hotA. tyAne paLUn jAyache dhADas kele nAhI
As policeman was watching he did not dare
पोलिस त्याला बारकाईने बघत असल्यामुळे त्याने पळून जायचे धाडस केले नाही
polis tyAlA bArakAIne baghat asalyAmuLe tyAne paLUn jAyache dhADas kele nAhI

blocks.heading: Example 4 – असल्या सारखा
rows: I had illusion of monsters coming
मोठे राक्षस येत आहेत असा मला भास झाला
moThe rAkShas yet Ahet asA malA bhAs jhAlA
मोठे राक्षस येत असल्या सारखा मला भास झाला
moThe rAkShas yet asalyA sArakhA malA bhAs jhAlA

title: Commands Imperative Statement - आज्ञार्थी वाक्य
content:
Like Hindi saying the verb itself is giving a command in Marathi.
While giving
the command
to many people or to a person with respect;
the verb
is suffixed withआ ( A).
Examples will explain it better.
ये पढ ( ye paDh )
हे वाच ( he vAch )
ये पढो/ पढिये ( ye paDho/ paDhiye )
हे वाचा ( he vAchA )
उसे कहानी बता ( use kahAnI batA )
त्याला गोष्ट सांग ( tyAlA goShT sAMg )
उसे कहानी बताओ / बतायिये ( use kahAnI batAo / batAyiye )
त्याला गोष्ट सांगा ( tyAlA goShT sAMgA )
Verbs ending with आ ( A) remain as it is when giving commands with respect
आप खाइये (Ap khAiye)
तुम्ही खा ( tumhI khA )
आप जाओ (Ap jAo )
तुम्ही जा ( tumhI jA )
Some verbs which are not ending with “अ”(a); suffixing “आ (A) will create bit different form.
See below examples of दे(de), पी (pI)
पानी पी ( pAnI pI )
पाणी पी ( pANI pI )
पानी पीयो / पीजिये ( pAnI pIyo / pIjiye )
पाणी प्या ( pANI pyA )
मुझे चाय दे ( mujhe chAy de )
मला चहा दे ( malA chahA de )
मुझे चाय दो / दीजिये ( mujhe chAy do / dIjiye )
मला चहा द्या ( malA chahA dyA )

title: Commands / Imperative statements in Marathi
content: Commands / Imperative statements in Marathi
intro: With तुम्ही (tumhI), verb is suffixed with आ (A). Negative: Verb+ऊ नकोस / नका (U nakos/nakA).

blocks.heading: Commands
blocks.content: Just saying the verb gives an order. With respect (तुम्ही tumhI), suffix आ (A). Verbs not ending with अ(a) may change form (e.g. दे de -> द्या dyA, पी pI -> प्या pyA).

blocks.heading: Negative commands
blocks.content: Verb+ऊ नकोस (U nakos) for तू. Verb+ऊ नका (U nakA) with respect.

blocks.heading: Verbs ending with आ (A)
blocks.content: Remain as-is with respect: तुम्ही खा (tumhI khA), तुम्ही जा (tumhI jA). For formal instructions, refer lesson "Giving instructions formally".

title: Comparison and degrees of comparison
content: Comparison and degrees of comparison
intro: In English there are three degrees: positive, comparative, superlative. In Marathi adjectives do not change; only degree words are prefixed: जास्त/अधिक for comparative, सर्वात/सर्वात जास्त/सर्वाधिक for superlative.

blocks.heading: Comparative sentence structure
blocks.content: Subject + सामान्य रूप + पेक्षा (pekShA) + जास्त/अधिक + adjective + form of असणे. जास्त/अधिक can be omitted while speaking.

blocks.heading: Superlative sentence structure
blocks.content: Subject + सर्वात/सर्वात जास्त/सर्वाधिक + adjective + form of असणे.

blocks.heading: Superlative examples
rows: Your voice is loudest of all
तुझा आवाज सर्वात जास्त मोठा आहे
tujhA AvAj sarvAt jAst moThA Ahe

title: Comparison in Marathi तुलना tulanA
content:
To compare between two-person or objects, In Hindi, we say, वह मेरेसे बडा है (Vah merese baDA hai), मै उससे लम्बा हूं (mai usase lambA hUM), वह सबसे मोटा है (vah sabase moTA hai) etc.
In Marathi sentence structure is same.
The word पेक्षा(pekShA) is appended to word with whom you are comparing subject.
The words like जास्त / अधिक (jAst/adhik) can be used to indicate more. But generally, these words can be omitted.

title: Compound verbs in Marathi
content: Compound verbs in Marathi
intro: Verbs देणे, टाकणे, घेणे, बसणे, काढणे, राहणे, जाणे, म्हणणे, ठेवणे, चुकणे, येणे are used with other verbs to emphasize or specify meaning. Structure: Main verb+ऊन/त/आयचा then form of compound verb.

blocks.heading: 1) टाकणे – not interested but doing (verb+ऊन)
rows: दहा मिनिटात संपवून टाक आणि जा झोपायला
dahA miniTAt saMpavUn TAk ANi jA jhopAyalA
Do not hesitate. Speak it out
Nobody is looking. Just do it
कोणी आपल्याकडे बघत नाहिये. करून टाक लवकर
koNI ApalyAkaDe baghat nAhiye. karUn TAk lavakar
I somehow managed to gulp it

blocks.heading: 2) देणे
blocks.content: Used in 'Let him play' etc. See lesson Using 'Let' and 'Shall we'.

blocks.heading: 3) घेणे – use opportunity (verb+ऊन)
rows: He quickly came and read the letter
तेवढ्यात त्याने येऊन पत्र वाचून घेतले
tevaDhyAt tyAne yeUn patr vAchUn ghetale
Ask whatever you want; right now
तुला काय विचारायचे आहे ते आत्ताच विचारून घे
tulA kAy vichArAyache Ahe te AttAch vichArUn ghe
You may call me whatever you want
तुला हवं ते तू बोलून घे
tulA havM te tU bolUn ghe
Get that work done by him
I made her take exercise every day
मी तीच्याकडून रोज व्यायाम करून घेतला
mI tIchyAkaDUn roj vyAyAm karUn ghetalA

blocks.heading: 4) काढणे – done vigorously (verb+ऊन)
rows: Batsman hit every ball very hard

blocks.heading: 5a) बसणे/जाणे – unintentionally (verb+ऊन)
rows: I just told him we drank liquor
मी त्यांना सांगून बसलो/गेलो की आपण काल दारू प्यायली
mI tyAMnA sAMgUn basalo/gelo kI ApaN kAl dArU pyAyalI
Oh my God! What have I done!
अरे देवा मी हे काय करून बसलो/गेलो
are devA mI he kAy karUn basalo/gelo

blocks.heading: 5b) बसणे – no option (verb+त)
rows: Now she will search it forever
आता ती कायम ते शोधत बसेल
AtA tI kAyam te shodhat basel

blocks.heading: 8) म्हणणे – predict consequences (verb+आयचा म्हटला तर)
rows: If we think of eating mango then..
If he decides to take revenge..
जर त्याने सूड घ्यायचा म्हटला तर कोणीही त्याला थांबवू शकत नाही
jar tyAne sUD ghyAyachA mhaTalA tar koNIhI tyAlA thAMbavU shakat nAhI

blocks.heading: 10) चुकणे – completed, hard to revert (verb+ऊन)
rows: I had already given my password
मी आधीच पासवर्ड सांगून चुकलो होतो
mI AdhIch pAsavarD sAMgUn chukalo hoto
I have decided a plan for tonight
मी आजचा प्लॅन करून चुकलो आहे
mI AjachA pl~an karUn chukalo Ahe

blocks.heading: 11) येणे – happened casually (verb+ण्यात आले)
rows: He happened to say he was from India
त्याच्या बोलण्यात आले की तोही भारतामधील आहे
tyAchyA bolaNyAt Ale kI tohI bhAratAmadhIl Ahe
I happened to hear about strike

title: Compound verbs / Phrases in Marathi
content: Compound verbs / Phrases in Marathi
intro: Verbs देणे, टाकणे, घेणे, बसणे, काढणे, राहणे, जाणे, म्हणणे, ठेवणे, चुकणे, येणे are used with other verbs to emphasize or specify meaning.

blocks.heading: 1) टाकणे – not interested but doing
rows: दहा मिनिटात संपवून टाक आणि जा झोपायला
dahA miniTAt saMpavUn TAk ANi jA jhopAyalA
Do not hesitate. Speak it out
Nobody is looking. Just do it
कोणी आपल्याकडे बघत नाहिये. करून टाक लवकर
koNI ApalyAkaDe baghat nAhiye. karUn TAk lavakar
I somehow managed to gulp it

blocks.heading: 2) देणे
blocks.content: Used in 'Let him play' etc. See lesson Using 'Let' and 'Shall we'.

blocks.heading: 3) घेणे – use opportunity
rows: He quickly came and read the letter
तेवढ्यात त्याने येऊन पत्र वाचून घेतले
tevaDhyAt tyAne yeUn patr vAchUn ghetale
Ask whatever you want; right now
तुला काय विचारायचे आहे ते आत्ताच विचारून घे
tulA kAy vichArAyache Ahe te AttAch vichArUn ghe
You may call me whatever you want
तुला हवं ते तू बोलून घे
tulA havM te tU bolUn ghe
Get that work done by him
I made her take exercise every day
मी तीच्याकडून रोज व्यायाम करून घेतला
mI tIchyAkaDUn roj vyAyAm karUn ghetalA

blocks.heading: 4) काढणे – done vigorously
rows: Batsman hit every ball very hard

blocks.heading: 5a) बसणे/जाणे – unintentionally
rows: I just told him we drank liquor
मी त्यांना सांगून बसलो की आपण काल दारू प्यायली
mI tyAMnA sAMgUn basalo kI ApaN kAl dArU pyAyalI
Oh my God! What have I done!
अरे देवा मी हे काय करून बसलो
are devA mI he kAy karUn basalo

blocks.heading: 6) राहणे – missed action
rows: She could not see the sign

blocks.heading: 7) जाणे – start & continue
rows: If you had taken exercise every day then
जर तू रोज व्यायाम करत गेला असतास तर
jar tU roj vyAyAm karat gelA asatAs tar
I was about to open the door when I saw
मी दरवाजा उघडायला गेलो तेवढ्यात मला पोलिस दिसले
mI daravAjA ughaDAyalA gelo tevaDhyAt malA polis disale

blocks.heading: 8) म्हणणे – predict consequences
rows: If we think of eating mango then
If he decides to take revenge
जर त्याने सूड घ्यायचा म्हटला तर कोणीही त्याला थांबवू शकत नाही
jar tyAne sUD ghyAyachA mhaTalA tar koNIhI tyAlA thAMbavU shakat nAhI

blocks.heading: 10) चुकणे – completed, hard to revert
rows: I had already given my password
मी आधीच पासवर्ड सांगून चुकलो होतो
mI AdhIch pAsavarD sAMgUn chukalo hoto
I have decided plan for tonight
मी आजचा प्लॅन करून चुकलो आहे
mI AjachA pl~an karUn chukalo Ahe

blocks.heading: 11) येणे – happened casually
rows: He happened to say he was from India
त्याच्या बोलण्यात आले की तोही भारतामधील आहे
tyAchyA bolaNyAt Ale kI tohI bhAratAmadhIl Ahe
I happened to hear about strike

title: Conjunction in Marathi उभयान्वयी अव्यय - Part 1 - और,लेकिन,फिर भी,क्योंकी ,इसलिये,कि aur,lekin,phir bhI,kyoMkI,isaliye,ki
content:
Conjunctions are words that join two or more words or phrases or clauses.
Here we will learn frequently used Hindi conjunctions and their Marathi synonyms.
We will start with frequently use conjunctions and then move to less-used ones

title: Conjunction in Marathi उभयान्वयी अव्यय - Part 2 - जैसा,या,अगर-तो,बादमें,फिर,जब तक-तब तक jaisA,yA,agar-to,phir,jab tak-tab tak
content:
Like in Hindi depending on gender and multiplicity of object form of जसा( jasA) will change
जसा-जशी-जसे-जसे-जश्या-जशी ( jasA-jashI-jase-jase-jashyA-jashI) as per
आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule
e.g.
जैसी आपकी इच्छा, सहाब (jaisI ApakI ichChA, sahAb )
जशी आपली इच्छा, साहेब ( jashI ApalI ichChA, sAheb )
वह जैसे जर रहा है वैसेही कर (vah jaise jar rahA hai vaisehI kar )
तो जसे करतो आहे तसेच कर (to jase karato Ahe tasech kar )

title: Conjunction in Marathi उभयान्वयी अव्यय - Part 3 जैसे ही,तब,के बजाय,ताकी,जबकि,नही तो,भी jaise hI ,tab,ke bajAy,tAkI,jabaki,nahi to,bhI
content:
The suffix ल्या is appended to past tense form of verb and it is said twice.
जैसे ही वह आता है मुझे बताना (jaise hI vah AtA hai mujhe batAnA )
तो आल्या आल्या मला सांग (to AlyA AlyA malA sAMg)
जैसे ही वह देखती है वह भाग गया (jaise vah dekhatI hai vah bhAg gayA )
तीने बघितल्या बघितल्या तो पळाला (tIne baghitalyA baghitalyA to paLAlA)
जैसे ही वह होंगे …( jaise hI vah hoMge …)
ते झाल्या झाल्या … (te jhAlyA jhAlyA …)
Other
way is to append “ताच”(tAch) to
verb
stem.
जैसे ही वह आता है मुझे बताना (jaise hI vah AtA hai mujhe batAnA )
तो येताच मला सांग (to yetAch malA sAMg)
जैसे ही वह देखती है वह भाग गया (jaise vah dekhatI hai vah bhAg gayA )
तीने बघताच तो पळाला (tIne baghatAch to paLAlA)
जैसे ही वह होंगे …( jaise hI vah hoMge …)
ते होताच … (te hotAch …)

title: Conjunctions in Marathi - Part 1 And,But,Because,So,That,Although,Though,Still,Yet
content: Conjunctions in Marathi – Part 1
intro: And, But, Although, Because, So, That. आणि/व, पण/परंतु, तरीही, कारण, म्हणून, की.

blocks.heading: 2) But – पण, परंतु
rows: I want to buy but shop is closed
मला खरेदी करायची आहे पण दुकान बंद आहे
malA kharedI karAyachI Ahe paN dukAn baMd Ahe
He was clever but did not work hard
तो हुशार होता पण त्याने परिश्रम केले नाहीत
to hushAr hotA paN tyAne parishram kele nAhIt

blocks.heading: 3) Although/Though/Still – तरीही
rows: I had warned him, still he opened the box
मी त्याला इशारा दिला होता तरीही त्याने खोका उघडला
mI tyAlA ishArA dilA hotA tarIhI tyAne khokA ughaDalA
He helped her still she insulted him
त्याने तिला मदत केली तरीही तीने त्याचा अपमान केला
tyAne tilA madat kelI tarIhI tIne tyAchA apamAn kelA

blocks.heading: 4) Because – कारण
rows: I did not come yesterday, because I was sick
मी काल आलो नाही, कारण मी आजारी होतो
mI kAl Alo nAhI, kAraN mI AjArI hoto
Car will not start because no petrol
गाडी चालू होणार नाही, कारण टाकीत पेट्रोल नाही
gADI chAlU hoNAr nAhI, kAraN TAkIt peTrol nAhI

blocks.heading: 5) So – म्हणून
rows: I was sick so I did not come
मी आजारी होतो म्हणून मी काल आलो नाही
mI AjArI hoto mhaNUn mI kAl Alo nAhI
No petrol so car will not start
टाकीत पेट्रोल नाही म्हणून गाडी चालू होणार नाही
TAkIt peTrol nAhI mhaNUn gADI chAlU hoNAr nAhI

blocks.heading: 6) That – की
rows: He told her that he is happy
त्याने तीला सांगितले की तो खूप खूष आहे
tyAne tIlA sAMgitale kI to khUp khUSh Ahe
I am sure that we will succeed
मला खात्री आहे की आपण यशस्वी होऊ
malA khAtrI Ahe kI ApaN yashasvI hoU

title: Conjunctions in Marathi - Part 2 As,As well as,Or, Neither, Nor, If,in case, provided
content: Conjunctions in Marathi – Part 2
intro: As, As well as, Or, Neither-Nor, If. जसा-जशी-जसे, तसेच/आणि-सुद्धा, किंवा. If: जर-तर (see If-Then lesson).

blocks.heading: 7) As – जसा (जसा-जशी-जसे-जसे-जश्या-जशी)
rows: Do it the same way as he is doing
तो जसे करतो आहे तसेच कर
to jase karato Ahe tasech kar

blocks.heading: 8) As well as – तसेच, आणि-सुद्धा
rows: तो तसेच मी / तो आणि मी सुद्धा
to tasech mI / to ANi mI suddhA
She sang as well as danced

blocks.heading: 10) Neither-Nor – convert to both
rows: Neither he nor I were interested
तो आणि मी दोघेही इच्छुक नव्हतो
to ANi mI doghehI ichChuk navhato

blocks.heading: 11) If / in case / provided
blocks.content: जर-तर (jar-tar). See lesson Sentence using If-Then.

title: Conjunctions in Marathi - Part 3 After, then,As far as, As long as,As if, As though
content: Conjunctions in Marathi – Part 3
intro: After/then, Till/Until/As long as, As far as, As if/as though. नंतर, जोपर्यंत-तोपर्यंत, जेवढे-त्याप्रमाणे, जणू.

blocks.heading: 12) After, then – नंतर, त्यानंतर, मग
rows: I finished study then I went to play
मी अभ्यास संपवला त्यानंतर खेळायला गेलो
mI abhyAs saMpavalA tyAnaMtar kheLAyalA gelo
I will call you then you enter on stage
मी तुला बोलवीन त्यानंतर/मग तू स्टेज वर ये
mI tulA bolavIn tyAnaMtar/mag tU sTej var ye

blocks.heading: 13) Till, Until, As long as – जोपर्यंत-तोपर्यंत
rows: As long as you want me, I will stay
जोपर्यंत तुला हवे आहे, तोपर्यंत मी तुझ्याबरोबर राहीन
joparyMt tulA have Ahe, toparyaMt mI tujhyAbarobar rAhIn
As long as you follow rules
जोपर्यंत तुम्ही नियम पाळता तोपर्यंत तुम्हाला काही अडचण येणार नाही
joparyMt tumhI niyam pALatA toparyaMt tumhAlA kAhI aDachaN yeNAr nAhI

blocks.heading: 14) As far as – जेवढे-त्याप्रमाणे
rows: As far as I know, he is studious
जेवढे मला माहिती आहे त्याप्रमाणे तो अभ्यासू व्यक्ती आहे
jevaDhe malA mAhitI Ahe tyApramANe to abhyAsU vyaktI Ahe

blocks.heading: 15) As far as __ concerned – जोपर्यंत ___चा प्रश्न आहे
rows: As far as India is concerned
जोपर्यंत भारताचा प्रश्न आहे, कंपनी चांगले काम करत आहे
joparyMt bhAratAchA prashn Ahe toparyaMt kaMpanI chAMgale kAm karat Ahe
As far as literacy is concerned
जोपर्यंत साक्षरतेचा प्रश्न आहे, आपल्याला खूप मेहनत करावी लागेल
joparyMt sAkSharatechA prashn Ahe ApalyAlA khUp mehanat karAvI lAgel

blocks.heading: 16) As if, as though – जणू, जणू काही
rows: He is blaming me as if I have not tried
तो माझ्यावर असे आरोप करत आहे, जणू मी प्रयत्नच केले नाहीत
to mAjhyAvar ase Arop karat Ahe, jaNU mI prayatnach kele nAhIt
He passed by as if she was not there
तो तिला स्मितहास्य न देता गेला, जणू ती तिथे नव्हतीच
to tilA smitahAsy na detA gelA, jaNU tI tithe navhatIch

title: Conjunctions in Marathi Part-4 As Soon as, Once,Rather Than, Instead of,So that,Whereas,Whether or not,also
content: Conjunctions in Marathi – Part 4
intro: As soon as, Once, Rather than, Instead of, So that, Whereas, Whether, Otherwise, Also. ल्या ल्या / ताच, की, ऐवजी, जेणेकरून, उलट.

blocks.heading: 17) As soon as – ल्या ल्या or ताच
18) Once – की
rows: Let me know once he comes
Once I see your story I will give review
मी तुझी गोष्ट बघितली की अभिप्राय देईन
mI tujhI goShT baghitalI kI abhiprAy deIn

blocks.heading: 19) Rather than, Instead of – ऐवजी
rows: Rather than surrendering soldier will accept death
I will take tea rather than coffee

blocks.heading: 20) So that – जेणेकरून
rows: I will work hard so that I will win
मी परिश्रम करीन जेणेकरून मी यशस्वी होईन
mI parishram karIn jeNekarUn mI yashasvI hoIn
We should avoid plastic so that less pollution
आपण प्लास्टिक टाळले पाहिजे जेणेकरून प्रदूषण कमी होईल
ApaN plAsTik TALale pAhije jeNekarUn pradUShaN kamI hoIl

blocks.heading: 21) Whereas – उलट, उलटपक्षी, प्रत्यक्षात
rows: He cheated me whereas I was helping him
त्याने मला फसवले उलट मी त्याला मदत करत होतो
tyAne malA phasavale ulaT mI tyAlA madat karat hoto
Govt said prices declined whereas inflation rose
सरकारने सांगितले की भाव कमी झाले उलट महागाई वाढली
sarakArane sAMgitale kI bhAv kamI jhAle ulaT mahAgAI vADhalI

blocks.heading: 22) Whether – का / का नाही
rows: I did not know whether he is going
तो जाणार आहे का मला माहित नाही
to jANAr Ahe kA malA mAhit nAhI
She could not realize whether to speak or not
तीने बोलावे का नाही तीला कळले नाही
tIne bolAve kA nAhI tIlA kaLale nAhI

blocks.heading: 23) Otherwise – नाहीतर
rows: Do this otherwise you will not win
हे कर नाहीतर तू जिंकणार नाहीस
he kar nAhItar tU jiMkaNAr nAhIs
He should inform me otherwise I will not know
त्याने मला लगेच कळवले पाहिजे नाहीतर मला कळणार नाही
tyAne malA lagech kaLavale nAhItar malA kaLaNAr nAhI

blocks.heading: 24) Also, as well – सुद्धा, ही
rows: He will come tomorrow as well

title: Connect two sentences जब-तब जो-वो जहां-वहा jab-tab jo-vo jahAM-vahA etc.
content:
From the title, it might not be clear what am I going to teach in this article. An example will make it clear what I mean by this title.
In Hindi we say “जो करेगा वो भरेगा”(jo karegA vo bharegA), जैसा तू बोलेगा वैसा मैं करूंगा (jaisA tU bolegA vaisA maiM karUMgA),जहां जाओ वहां खुशिया है (jahAM jAo vahAM khushiyA hai)
Here we connected two parts using word pairs like जो-वो(jo-vo), जैसा-वैसा(jaisA-vaisA), जहां-वहां (jahAM-vahAM) etc.
The sentence structure is similar in Marathi.
So when we are using “तो” in the post-condition part we will use its companion viz. “जो” in condition part.
So when we are using “ते” in the post-condition part we will use its companion viz. “जे” in condition part.
e.g.
तूने अभी जो कहा वो मुझे समझा नही ( tUne abhI jo kahA vo mujhe samajhA nahI )
तू आत्ता जे म्हणालास ते मला कळले नाही (tU AttA je mhaNAlAs te malA kaLale nAhI )
In Marathi also we have such pairs, like – जो-तो, जी-ती, जे-ते (jo-to, jI-tI, je-te) etc.
By changing the first letter to “ज”(j) you can make companion word.
e.g word is “वहां” replace व(v) by ज”(j). It becomes. जहां (jahAM)
e.g word is “वैसी” replace व(v) by ज”(j). It becomes. जैसी (jaisI)
Below I have listed such pairs :
Pronoun/word in Hindi
Pronoun/word in Marathi
Its companion in “condition Marathi
वह/वो (पु.)  (vah)
तो / तो / हा
जो (jo)
वह/वो (स्त्री.) (vah)
ती / ती / ही
जी  (jI)
वह/वो (नपु.) (vah)
ते / ते / हे
जे (je)
वे(पु.)  (ve)
ते / ते / हे
जे (je)
वे(स्त्री.)  (ve)
त्या / त्या / ह्या
ज्या (jyA)
वे(नपु.)  (ve)
ती / ती / ही
जी (jI)
तब/तभी  (ve)
तेव्हा (tevhA)
जेव्हा  (jevhA)
वैसा (vaisA)
तसा (tasA)
जसा (jasA )
उतने (utane)
तितके (titake)
जितके (jitake)
उतने (utane)
तेवढे (tevaDhe)
जेवढे (jevaDhe)
वहां (vahAM)
तिथे (tithe)
जिथे (jithe)

title: Conversation about the eclipse
content: Conversation about the eclipse
intro: Kaushik and friend discuss solar eclipse, goggles, lunar eclipse, cultural beliefs (Chinese dog, Rahu-Ketu), Indian astronomy.

blocks.heading: Eclipse conversation
rows: Kaushik, why are you going to terrace?
I am going to watch the solar eclipse
Solar eclipse should not be seen by naked eyes
सूर्यग्रहण नुसत्या डोळ्यांनी बघायचं नसतं असं ऐकलं आहे
sUryagrahaN nusatyA DoLyAMnI baghAyachM nasatM asM aikalM Ahe
Do you have special goggle to watch the eclipse?
तुझ्याकडे ग्रहण बघायचा खास चष्मा आहे का?
tujhyAkaDe grahaN baghAyachA khAs chaShmA Ahe kA
Because of moon coming between sun and earth, sun gets hidden
सूर्य आणि पृथ्वीच्या मध्ये चंद्र आल्यामुळे सूर्य झाकला जातो
sUry ANi pRuthvIchyA madhye chMdr AlyAmuLe sUry jhAkalA jAto
Lunar eclipse can be seen by naked eyes
In Chinese culture, heavenly dog eats the sun
चिनी संस्कृतीमध्ये, आकाशातला कुत्रा सूर्याला खातो अशी कल्पना आहे
chinI sMskRutImadhye AkAshAtalA kutrA sUryAlA khAto ashI kalpanA Ahe
In India, Rahu and Ketu gulp the sun and moon
भारतात राहू आणि राक्षस सूर्य आणि चंद्राला गिळतात अशी कल्पना आहे
bhAratAt rAhU ANi rAkShas sUry ANi chMdrAlA giLatAt ashI kalpanA Ahe
Eclipse will start in five minutes

title: Daily Marathi reading practise ... Marathi jokes, thoughts, quotes
content: Daily Marathi reading practise ... Marathi jokes, thoughts, quotes
intro: Daily Marathi reading practise with jokes, thoughts, and quotes from 2020. Topics: social media, Diwali, travel, vegetables, corona, life lessons.

blocks.heading: Sample entries
blocks.content: Entries are dated (e.g. 9 Feb 2020, 6 Dec 2020). Each has Marathi text with transliteration and English translation. Topics include: New year resolution (no Facebook), Pappu-Mini joke (remote control car), astrologer joke (36 gun match), Ganya-Sakhya (snake/stick), essay (माझे आई-वडील), sweet tongue quote, Bandya joke (job selection), Chintu pampering, fat lady thief, exam dreams, bronchitis doctor, corona government, rickshaw class, eye doctor, CCTV, result-sanitizer, lottery-kidnap, racer car, Teachers day, mask elastic, etc.

blocks.heading: Sample quotes & jokes
rows: From the new year, I have decided not to spend time on Facebook
नवीन वर्षापासून मी ठरवलंय ... फेसबुक वर अजिबात वेळ घालवायचा नाही
navIn varShApAsUn mI TharavalMy ... Facebook var ajibAt veL ghAlavAyachA nAhI
If your eyes are good, you will fall in love of this world. But if your tongue is sweet, this whole world will fall in your love
जर तुमचे डोळे चांगले असतील तर तुम्ही या जगाच्या प्रेमात पडाल. पण तुमची जीभ गोड असेल तर हे संपूर्ण जग तुमच्या प्रेमात पडेल
jar tumache DoLe chAMgale asatIl tar tumhI yA jagAchyA premAt paDAl. paN tumachI jIbh goD asel tar he sMpUrN jag tumachyA premAt paDel
A relationship created by pure mind will never break
निर्मळ मानाने बनलेलं नातं कधीच तुटणार नाही
nirmaL mAnAne banalelM nAtM kadhIch tuTaNAr nAhI
Look at every problem as an opportunity
आयुष्यात येणाऱ्या प्रत्येक समस्येला सामोरे जाताना त्या समस्येकडे एक संधी म्हणून बघा
AyuShyAt yeNAryA pratyek samasyelA sAmore jAtAnA tyA samasyekaDe ek sMdhI mhaNUn baghA
I am not trying to be better than others but better than what I was yesterday
माझा प्रयत्न इतरांपेक्षा श्रेष्ठ होणे नसून मी जो काल होतो त्यापेक्षा आज चांगला होण्याचा आहे
mAjhA prayatn itarAMpekShA shreShTh hoNe nasUn mI jo kAl hoto tyApekShA Aj chAMgalA hoNyAchA Ahe

title: Difference between नाही(nAhI) and नको (nako)
content: Difference between नाही(nAhI) and नको (nako)
intro: नाही (nAhI) = general negation. नको (nako) = only for imperative (requests, suggestions, advice, commands).

blocks.heading: नको (nako)
blocks.content: Used in negative imperative: Commands, Using To Want/To Need, Using "Let" and "Shall we".

blocks.heading: नाही (nAhI)
blocks.content: Used in: Negative Present/Past/Future tense, Using "To Like" with other verbs, and all other negative sentences.

title: Difference in Hindi and Marathi pronunciation
content:
Both Marathi and Hindi languages used the same writing script which is Devanagari. So if you can read and write Hindi then it means you can read and write Marathi.
There are only a few exceptions where Marathi pronunciation is different than that of Hindi for symbol. I have listed below such cases

title: Exceptional Verbs in Marathi which change in past tense form
content: Exceptional Verbs in Marathi which change in past tense form
intro: Some verbs change themselves before creating past tense form. As past tense is the base of Perfect tenses, it is important to know these forms.

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
By now we have studied many grammar concepts including tense. The forms of the verb in simple past tense are the base of perfect tenses and other phrases. We have also observed that few verbs behave differently when their past tense form is created.

As per rule we append लो(lo) to verb for "I". e.g. बोल(bol)+लो(lo)=बोललो(bolalo), हस(has)+लो(lo)=हसलो(hasalo). So I spoke -> मी बोललो, I laughed -> मी हसलो.

But I went -> मी गेलो(mI gelo). The verb is जाणे(jANe), so we would expect जा(jA)+लो(lo)=जालो(jAlo). But we appended suffix to गे(ge) to make गेलो(gelo). This is the unusual behavior: verbs which change themselves before creating past tense form.

blocks.heading: Exceptional verbs: changed form and examples
rows: I did it: मी तो केला
tried learning French: फ्रेंच शिकायचा प्रयत्न केला
lived in city: मी शहरात राहिलो
asked for his pen: त्याचे पेन मागितले
he ran fast: तो जोराने पळाला

blocks.heading: Notes
blocks.content:
**Similar to "To Try":** Many verbs use करणे (karaNe), e.g. व्यायाम करणे, आंघोळ करणे. In all such verbs, the form of करणे will be created as per above rule (के).

**For म्हणणे (mhaNaNe):** Both म्हटला (mhaTalA) and म्हणला (mhaNalA) are used.

title: Exceptional Verbs in Marathi which change in past tense form
content:
By now we have studied many grammar concepts including tense. We have seen the forms of the verb in the simple past tense is base of perfect tenses and other phrases.
We have also observed that few verbs behave differently when their past tense form is created.
As per rule, we append लो(lo) to the verb to create its past tense form for “I”.
e.g.
I spoke -> मी बोललो ( mI bolalo )
I laughed -> मी हसलो ( mI hasalo )
i.e.
बोल (bol)+ लो (lo) = बोललो (bolalo )
हस(has)+ लो (lo) = हसलो ( hasalo )
But,
I went -> मी गेलो( mI gelo ).
Now verb is “जाणे”(jANe). so we should have said जा(jA) + लो(lo)= जालो (jAlo ).
But we did not say say. We appended suffix to गे(ge) to make it गेलो(gelo).
This is what I want to point out ! The unusual behavior of few verbs. !! The verbs which change themselves before creating past tense form. As past tense form is base of many other tenses and phrases it is important to know such form.
Refer below link for list of such verbs and their irregular simple past tense form.
Exceptional Verbs in Marathi which change in past tense form

title: Exclamations in Hindi and Marathi
content:
Exclamations or विस्मयादिबोधक are an attractive part of any language. It conveys feeling with added weight. In English, we say “Oh My God!!” , “Oh No!! ” “How beautiful” etc. In Hindi, we say “हे भगवान”(he bhagavAn), कितना सुंदर (kitanA suMdar) etc.
The Marathi language also has many exclamations.
The Marathi language has many dialects and different styles of speaking based on the region in Maharashtra. Each dialect can have its special exclamations.
Below I have listed exclamation which is widely used in all the dialects.
Not all Marathi exclamations can have similar words in Hindi and vice-versa. Try to understand the example to know it better.
To Express surprise आश्चर्य
1) खरंच ! ( kharaMch )
Similar to “सच !” or “सचमें !!” in Hindi
सचमें ! मैने वैसे कहा ( sachameM ! maine vaise kahA )
खरंच !! मी तसं बोललो (kharaMch mI tasaM bolalo )
2) अरे ! (are !) / अरेच्चा (arechchA!) / अरेच्च्या (arechchyA !)
Similar to अरे ! in Hindi, to indicate surprise.
अरे ! तुम कब आयें (are ! tum kab AyeM)
अरे !/ अरेच्चा/ अरेच्च्या तुम्ही कधी आलात ( are !/ arechchA !/ arechchyA ! tumhI kadhI AlAt ? )
To express surprise of size. i.e. आकार के बारेमें आश्चर्य
3) अबब ! ( ababa !)
e.g. कितना बडा (kitanA baDA ) !! -> अबब ! किती मोठा ( ababa !! kitI moThA )
To express pleasant feeling. i.e. सुहावनी भावना
4) अहाहा ! ( ahAhA ! )
e.g. अच्छी खुशबू है !! ( acChA svAd hai ) -> अहाहा ! ( ahAhA ! )
अच्छा स्वाद है (acChA svAd hai ) -> -> अहाहा ! ( ahAhA ! )
To express sorrow i.e. दुःख
5) ओहो ! ( oho ! )
e.g. ओहो !बुरा हुआ ( oho! burA huA) -> ओहो ! वाईट झालं ( oho !! vAIT jhAlaM )
6) अरे देवा ! (are devaa ! )
Same as “हे भगवान”(he bhagavAn) in Hindi e.g.
हे भगवान ! ये किसने किया ( he bhagavAn !ye kisane kiyA)
अरे देवा ! हे कोणी केलं (are devaa ! he koNI kelM )
To express shock i.e. सदमा / धक्का
7) अरे बापरे (are baapare !)
same as in Hindi. e.g.
अरे बापरे ! वह फेल हो गया!! (are baapare ! vah phel ho gayA)
अरे बापरे ! तो नापास झाला ( are baapare ! to nApAs jhAlA )
To express disapproval. नामंजूरी / नापसंदगी
8) छे! (Che!) / छट ! (ChaT !)</strong
Similar to “ना ना !” in Hindi. e.g.
ना ना ! वह ऐसा कभी नही करेगा (nA nA ! vah aisA kabhI nahI karegA)
छे! तो कधीही असं करणार नाही (Che! to kadhIhI asaM karaNAr nAhI )
ना ना ! ना ना !मेरा इसपे विश्वास नहीं (nA nA !merA isape vishvAs nahIM)
छट ! मझा नाही विश्वास (ChaT ! majhA nAhI vishvAs )
To express Approval i.e. मंजूरी / पसंदगी
9) होय ! (hoy !)
e.g. Similar to “हां ! ” in Hindi e.g.
हां ! मै जरूर आऊंगा ( hAM ! mai jarUr AUMgA)-> होय ! मी नक्की येणार (hoy mI nakkI yeNAr )
10) हं ( haM )
Same as हं ( haM ) in Hindi e.g.
हं! समझा मुझे ( haM !samajhA mujhe)
हं ! समजलं मला ते ( haM ! samajalM malA te )
To express appreciation i.e. प्रशंसा
11) शाब्बास ! ( shAbbAs )
Same as शाब्बाश (shAbbAsh ) in Hindi e.g.
शाब्बाश ! तूने मॅच जीत ली ( shAbbAsh ! tUne mach jIt lI )
शाब्बास ! तू सामना जिंकलास (shAbbAs ! tU sAmanA jiMkalAs )
12) अरे वा ! (are vA !) / छान ( ChAn !)
Same as अरे वा ! (are vA !), बढिया (baDhiyA) in Hindi
अरे वा ! / बढिया लिखावट (are vA ! likhAvaT)
अरे वा ! / छान ! हस्ताक्षर ( are vA ! / ChAn ! hastAkShar )
13) खूप छान ( khUp ChAn )
Same as बहुत अच्छे (bahut acChe ) in Hindi
14) मस्त ! ( mast !) / जबरदस्त ( jabaradast !)
Same in Hindi.
To express pain i.e दर्द
15) अगं आई /अगं आईगं ( agaM AI ! / agaM AIgaM! )
Same as “मा!” “हे मा!” in Hindi
हे मा! बहुत दर्द हो रहा है ( he mA! bahut dard ho rahA hai )
अगं आई /अगं आईगं खूप दुखतंय ( agaM AI ! / agaM AIgaM! khUp dukhataMy )
To express disgust i.e. घृणा
16) शी ! शीशी !! ( shI ! / shIshI ! )
same in Hindi. e.g.
शी ! कितना बदसूरत दिख रहा है ये (shI ! kitanA badasUrat dikha rahA hai ye)
शी ! / शीशी !! घाणेरडं दिसतंय ते ( shI ! / shIshI ! ghANeraDM disataMy te )
17) thU ! ( थू !)
Literally, sound of spitting. Same in Hindi. e.g.
थू ! तुम्हारे कायरता पे ( thU tumhAre kAyaratA pe )
थू ! तुमच्या भित्रेपणावर (thU ! tumachyA bhitrepaNAvar )To ask to keep quiet i.e. शांतता
18) शूऽऽऽ /श्श! (shU / shsh !!)
Exactly same in Hindi
शू /श्श! शांत बैठिये ! ( shU / shsh !! shAMt baiThiye !! )
शू /श्श! शांत बसा ! ( shU / shsh !! shAMt basA !! )
To give stress on any adjective we add कितना !! Similarly is in Marathi.
e.g. कितना सुंदर (kitanA suMdar) -> किती सुंदर (kitI suMdar )
कितना आश्चर्यजनक ( kitanA Ashcharyajanak ) -> काय आश्चर्य ! (kAy Ashcharya ! )
कितना अच्छा नाच किया तुमने ( kitanA achChA nAch kiyA tumane )
किती छान नाचलास तू ! (kitI ChAn nAchalAs tU !)
कितना मूर्ख है वो ( kitanA mUrkh hai vo )
काय मूर्ख आहे तो ! ( kAy mUrkha Ahe to !)

blocks.heading: Pleasant / Sorrow

blocks.heading: Shock / Disapproval

छे! तो कधीही असं करणार नाही
Che! to kadhIhI asaM karaNAr nAhI

title: Express wish in Marathi इच्छा आशिर्वाद
content:
In Hindi, we give blessing like भगवान तुम्हारा भला करे(bhagavAn tumhArA bhalA kare), ईश्वर तुम्हे सद्बुद्धी दे (Ishvar tumhe sadbuddhI de).
Or we express wish like दुकान जल्दी खुल जाये (dukAn jaldI khul jAye), वह ये काम कर डालें (vah ye kAm kar DAleM)
In Marathi, we say such wishes by appending ओ(o) for singular and ओत(ot) to plural.
e.g.
for verb do i.e. करणे(karaNe) forms are करो(karo) and करोत (karot)
for verb speak i.e. बोलणे(bolaNe) forms are बोलो(bolo) and बोलोत (bolot)
For verbs not ending with अ(a) we append वो(vo) for singular and वोत(vot) for plural.
for verb give i.e.देणे (deNe) forms are देवो ( devo ) and देवोत ( devot )
for verb become i.e होणे ( hoNe ) forms are होवो ( hovo ) and होवोत ( hovot )
भगवान तुम्हे आशिर्वाद दें ( bhagavAn tumhe AshirvAd deM )
देव तुम्हाला आशिर्वाद देवो ( dev tumhAlA AshirvAd devo )
वह तुम्हे मदत करें ( vah tumhe madat kareM )
तो तुम्हाला मदत करो ( to tumhAlA madat karo )
वे तुम्हे मदत करें ( ve tumhe madat kareM )
ते तुम्हाला मदत करोत ( te tumhAlA madat karot )
नया साल आपको सुखदायक हो (nayA sAl Apako sukhadAyak ho)
नवीन वर्ष सुखदायक असो ( navIn varSh sukhadAyak aso )
दुकान जल्दी खुल जाये (dukAn jaldI khul jAye)
दुकान लवकर उघडो ( dukAn lavakar ughaDo)
भगवान तुम्हारा भला करे(bhagavAn tumhArA bhalA kare)
देव तुमचं भलं करो ( dev tumachaM bhalaM karo )
In Marathi, we generally use this form only for third-person. Not with first or second person i.e. NOT with मी, आम्ही, तू, तुम्ही.
So In Hindi, we can form a sentence like तू मुझे मदत करें(tU mujhe madat kareM) which will be in Marathi as तू मला मदत करो ( tU malA madat karo ). So though grammatically perfect, it sounds bit irregular in Marathi.

title: Form of verbs (declensions) - miscellaneous
content:
In Hindi, we say खाके देख (khAke dekh), करके जा (karake jA ), गाके सुना (gAke sunA ). Here we have connected two verbs to indicate the sequence of action.
In Marathi, we will suffix ऊन (Un) to the first verb to indicate continuation.
बैठके देख ( baiThake dekh )
बसून बघ (basUn bagh)
आके जा (Ake jA )
येऊन जा (yeUn jA)
उसने आके देखा ( usane Ake dekhA )
त्याने येऊन बघितले (tyAne yeUn baghitale )
वह जाके जांचेंगे ( vah jAke jAMcheMge )
ते जाऊन तपासतील (te jAUn tapAsatIl)
हसके बोल ( hasake bol )
हसून बोल ( hasoon bol)

title: Forms of verb To Be होना-हूं-था-होऊंगा-है-होगा etc.
content:
The verb “होना” in Hindi is असणे (asaNe) in Marathi.
Like Hindi, depending on tense, gender and multiplicity the form changes.
There is no logic, why these forms are this way. o initially, you have to by-heart it. Practice will make you perfect.

title: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Marathi (Devanagari)
content: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Marathi (Devanagari)
intro: Listen to similar-sounding pairs to practice differentiating difficult consonants. Use the speaker buttons to hear each pair.

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
In the previous two lessons viz. **Vowels, Consonants in Marathi and their pronunciation** and **Marathi Barakahadi : Symbols for vowels with consonants**, you have seen Marathi (Devanagari) alphabets and their pronunciation.

But sometimes you may face difficulty in differentiating the sounds of two or more consonants. Especially if your native language does not have these consonants, it needs extra effort to sense and create these sounds.

Listening to Marathi a lot is the best way to improve. Also, one of my blog users – Vladimir from Russia – suggested that it is easy to learn when similar sounding words are spoken together, like a pair.

My other blog user – Priscilla from U.K. – gave me such a list for त, ट, ठ, थ, द, ढ. So in the corresponding video I have given pronunciation of these pairs. I hope it helps. Let me know if you find it useful and need more such pairs.

blocks.heading: Listen carefully to understand the difference between
blocks.content: ट and त, ठ and थ, द and ढ etc.

title: Frequently used sentences in Marathi - miscellaneous part 2
content: Frequently used sentences in Marathi – miscellaneous part 2
intro: Go by bus, I have a car, what have you prepared for lunch today.

blocks.heading: 1) I go by bus
blocks.content: Append ने(ne) to सामान्यरूप of the noun. बसने (basane), रिक्षाने (rikShAne).

blocks.heading: Transport sentences
rows: He walks to office / He goes by foot

blocks.heading: 2) I have a car
blocks.content: Refer lesson 'To have' to indicate possession and relation.

blocks.heading: 3) What have you prepared
rows: What have you prepared for lunch today?
What have you prepared for lunch today?
What have you prepared for breakfast today?

title: Frequently used sentences in Marathi - miscellaneous
content: Frequently used sentences in Marathi – miscellaneous
intro: Miscellaneous sentences used in different scenarios: hunger, thirst, favorite, waiting, permission, working condition, wearing, wish, belief.

blocks.heading: 1) Hunger & Thirst
blocks.content: Hunger -> भूक(bhUk); To be hungry -> भूक लागणे (bhUk lAgaNe). Thirst -> तहान(tahAn); To be thirsty -> तहान लागणे (tahAn lAgaNe). We use preposition 'To' with subject.

blocks.heading: 2) Favorite
blocks.content: Favorite -> आवडता (AvaDatA) / सर्वांत आवडता (sarvAMt AvaDatA). आवडता changes as per gender and multiplicity.

blocks.heading: 3) To wait for
blocks.content: To wait for ___ -> -ची वाट बघणे(-chI vAT baghaNe). Use possessive form: माझी, तुझी, त्याची, कौशिकची, etc.

blocks.heading: 4) Is it ok if I?
blocks.content: Use phrase 'चालेल का?' with If-then sentence.

blocks.heading: Permission examples
rows: Is it ok if I sit here?
मी इथे बसलो तर चालेल का?
mI ithe basalo tar chAlel kA?
Is it ok if he sits here?
तो इथे बसला तर चालेल का?
to ithe basala tar chAlel kA?
Is it ok if they play on terrace?
ते गच्चीवर खेळले तर चालेल का?
te gachchIvar kheLale tar chAlel kA?

blocks.heading: 5) Working / Not working
blocks.content: चालू असणे (chAlU asaNe) or सुरू असणे (surU asaNe) for 'is it working'. बंद असणे (baMd asaNe) for 'not working'.

blocks.heading: 6) To wear
blocks.content: To wear -> घालणे (ghAlaNe). In Marathi we use perfect tense for 'I am wearing' = 'I have worn'.

blocks.heading: Wearing sentences
rows: He had worn suit when he met me
तो मला भेटलो तेव्हा त्याने सूट घातला होता
to malA bheTalo tevhA tyAne sUT ghAtalA hotA
Wait, I am wearing tie (in act)
एक मिनिट थांब. मी टाय घालतो आहे
ek miniT thAMb. mI TAy ghAlato Ahe

blocks.heading: 7) Wish / To wish
blocks.content: Wish (noun) -> इच्छा (ichCA). To wish -> इच्छा असणे (ichCA asaNe). Similar to 'It is my wish that… may/should…'

blocks.heading: Wish sentences
rows: I wish he goes to school
माझी अशी इच्छा आहे की तो शाळेत गेला पाहिजे
mAjhI ashI ichChA Ahe kI to shALet gelA pAhije
त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की कौशिक जिंको
tyAchI ashI ichChA Ahe kI kaushik jiMko

blocks.heading: 8) To believe
blocks.content: विश्वास असणे (vishvAs asaNe) = already believing. विश्वास ठेवणे (vishvAs ThevaNe) = starting/willing to believe.

blocks.heading: Believe sentences
rows: तू कृपया माझ्यावर विश्वास ठेवशील का?
tU kRupayA mAjhyAvar vishvAs ThevashIl kA?

title: Future Continuous Tense in Marathi
content: Future Continuous Tense in Marathi
intro: Structure: Pronoun + Verb+त (t) + Form of असणे in Simple Future.

blocks.heading: Structure
blocks.content:
You have learned Simple Future tense of "to Be" i.e. असणे (asaNe). So you have almost done with Future Continuous Tense.

The sentence structure: Pronoun + Verb+त (t) + Form of असणे in Simple Future

**Example 1:** For I(male/female), form of असणे in Simple Future is असेन (asen). I will be walking -> मी चाल(chAl)+त असेन -> मी चालत असेन (mI chAlat asen)

**Example 2:** For "We", form is असू (asU). We will be walking -> आम्ही चाल(chAl)+त असू -> आम्ही चालत असू (AmhI chAlat asU)

title: Generalizing words - somewhere somehow anything anybody etc.तरी(tarI) -ही(hI)
content: Generalizing words – somewhere somehow anything anybody
intro: तरी(tarI) = some (someone, somehow); ही(hI) = any (anyone, anyhow). तरी as separate word = still/at least. ही = also/even.

blocks.heading: Full-sentence examples
rows: He is not ready to go anywhere

blocks.heading: तरी = still / at least
rows: I told him, still he ate it
मी त्याला सांगितले तरी त्याने हे खाल्ले
mI tyAlA sAMgitale tarI tyAne he khAlle
Finish work at least by tomorrow
You should have told me at least
तू मला तरी सांगायला हवे होतेस
tU malA tarI saMgAyalA have hotes

blocks.heading: ही = also / even
rows: He did not even tell it to me

blocks.heading: Indefinite Pronouns with Every
blocks.content: प्रत्येक (pratyek) = every. Use with noun.

title: Generalizing words - somewhere somehow anything anybody etc.तरी(tarI) -ही(hI)
content:
Consider the
conversation 1)
“Who did this mess ?”
“Someone did it?”
conversation 2)
“How will you do it?”
“I will manage it somehow”
When we are not sure about the thing we say “somehow”, “somewhere”, “anybody” etc. They are called indefinite pronouns.
In Marathi also such words are present.
In words like
“somehow”, “somewhere” we prefixed “some” to words like “how”, “where”
etc. For such phrases in Marathi, we append
तरी(tarI)
.
In words like
“anyhow”, “anywhere” we prefixed “any” to words like “how”, “where”
etc. For such phrases in Marathi, we append
ही(hI)
.
The below table lists such words.
While adding these prefixes there is little change in the main word. such words are highlighted.
who
कोण (koN)
someone / somebody
कोणीतरी  (koNItarI)
anyone / anybody
कोणीही (koNIhI)
when
कधी (kadhI)
sometime
कधीतरी (kadhItarI)
anytime
कधीही (kadhIhI)
when
केव्हा (kevhA)
sometime
केव्हातरी (kevhAtarI)
anytime
केव्हाही (kevhAhI)
where
कुठे (kuThe)
somewhere
कुठेतरी (kuThetarI)
anywhere
कुठेही (kuThehI)
what
काय (kAy)
something
काहीतरी (kAhItarI)
anything
काहीही (kAhIhI)
how much /
how many
किती (kitI)
many
कितीतरी (kitItarI)
any
कितीही (kitIhI)
how
कसा (kasA)
somehow
कसातरी (kasAtarI)
anyhow
कसाही (kasAhI)
which
कुठला (kuThalA)
someone/ something/ some
कुठलातरी (kuThalAtarI)
any
कुठलाही (kuThalAhI)
which
कोणता (koNatA)
someone/something
कोणतातरी (koNatAtarI)
any
कोणताही (koNatAhI)
To use a preposition with these words, the preposition is appended to the main word and then तरी(tarI) or ही(hI) is used.
e.g.
someone -> कोणीतरी (koNItarI)
to someone -> कोणाला तरी (koNAlA tarI)
some -> कुठलातरी (kuThalAtarI)
in which box -> कुठल्या खोक्यात (kuThalyA khokyAt )
into some box -> कुठल्या तरी खोक्यात (kuThalyA tarI khokyAt )
Full-sentence examples:
Call someone -> कोणाला तरी बोलव (koNAlA tarI bolav)
Call anyone -> कोणालाही बोलव (koNAlAhI bolav)
I reached there somehow -> मी तिकडे कसातरी पोचलो (mI tikaDe kasAtarI pochalo)
I will reach there anyhow -> मी तिकडे कसाही पोचेन (mI tikaDe kasAhI pochen )
He has gone somewhere from the morning -> तो सकाळपासून कुठेतरी गेला आहे (to sakALapAsUn kuThetarI gelA Ahe)
He is not ready to go anywhere -> तो कुठेही जायला तयार नाही (to kuThehI jAyalA tayAr nAhI )

title: Giving instructions formally
content: Giving instructions formally
intro: Formal instructions use आवा form (positive) or ऊ नये (negative). आवा follows आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई rule. For अकर्मक verbs use neuter-singular.

blocks.heading: Positive instructions
blocks.content: Append आवा/आवी/आवे etc. to verb. Forms: आवा–आवी–आवे/आवेत–आवे–आव्या/आव्यात–आवी/आवीत.

blocks.heading: Negative instructions
blocks.content: Add ऊ नये (U naye) to verb. For plurals sometimes नयेत (nayet).

title: Greeting,Wishes,Blessings,Slogans in Marathi - मराठीमें शुभकामनाएं/आशिर्वाद/नारे marAThImeM shubhakAmanAeM/AshirvAd/nAre
content:
Below I have summarized few frequently used Greetings, Wishes, Blessings and Slogans in Marathi. Please note that they are NOT a word to word translation from Hindi to Marathi. These are the ways how a native Marathi speaker will greet you on a particular occasion.
Please go through below:
शुभकामनाएं (shubhakAmanAeM)
शुभेच्छा (shubhechChA)
ढेर सारी शुभकामनाएं
(Dher sArI shubhakAmanAeM)
अनेकानेक शुभेच्छा
(anekAnek shubhechChA)
जन्मदिन की बधाई/शुभकामनाएं
(janmadina kI badhAI/shubhakAmanAeM)
वाढदिवसाच्या हार्दिक शुभेच्छा
(vADhadivasAchyA hArdik shubhechChA)
दिवाली की हार्दिक शुभकामनाएं
(divAlI kI hArdik shubhakAmanAeM) /
शुभ दीपावली  (shubha dIpAvalI)
दिवाळीच्या हार्दिक शुभेच्छा
(divaLIchyA hArdik shubhechChA)
क्रिसमस की शुभकामनाएं
(krisamasa kI shubhakAmanAeM )
नाताळच्या/ख्रिसमसच्या हार्दिक शुभेच्छा
(nAtALachyA/khrisamasachyA  hArdik shubhechChA)
___ की शुभकामनाएं
(___kI shubhakAmanAeM )
____च्या हार्दिक शुभेच्छा
(___chyA hArdik shubhechChA)
अभिनन्दन (abhinandan) / बधाई हो (badhAI ho)
अभिनंदन(abhinaMdan)
हार्दिक अभिनंदन(hArdik abhinaMdan)
हार्दिक/मनःपूर्वक अभिनंदन (hArdik/manHpUrvak abhinaMdan)
मैं तुमसे प्यार करता/करती हूं
(maiM tumase pyAr karatA/karatI hUM)
माझे तुझ्यावर प्रेम आहे
(mAjhe tujhyAvar prem Ahe)
आयुष्मान भव(AyuShmAn bhava)
आयुष्मान भव(AyuShmAn bhava)
जीवेत शरदः शतम(jIvet sharadH shatam)
जीवेत शरदः शतम(jIvet sharadH shatam)
यशस्वी भव(yashasvI bhava)
यशस्वी भव(yashasvI bhava)
चिरंजीव भव(chiraMjIv bhava)
चिरंजीव भव(chiraMjIv bhava)
अखंडसौभाग्यवती भव / सदासुहागन रहो
(akhaMDasaubhAgyavatI bhava /
sadAsuhAgan raho)
अखंडसौभाग्यवती भव
(akhaMDasaubhAgyavatI bhava)
आशिर्वाद(AshirvAd)
आशिर्वाद(AshirvAd)
मुझे आशिर्वाद दीजिए (mujhe AshirvAd dIjie)
मला आशिर्वाद द्या (malA AshirvAd dyA)
मेरे आशिर्वाद हंमेशा तुम्हारे साथ है
(mere AshirvAd haMmeshA tumhAre sAth hai)
माझे आशिर्वाद सदैव तुझ्या पाठीशी आहेत.
(mAjhe AshirvAd sadaiva tujhyA pAThIshI Ahet.)
धन्यवाद (dhanyavAd)
धन्यवाद(dhanyavAd)
भगवान तुम्हारा भला करे
(bhagavAn tumhArA bhalA kare)
देव तुझं भलं करो
(dev tujhaM bhalaM karo)
जिंदाबाद (jiMdAbAd)
जिंदाबाद(jiMdAbAd)
भारत जिन्दाबाद (bhArat jindAbAd)
भारत जिन्दाबाद(bhArat jindAbAd)
इन्किलाब जिन्दाबाद (inkilAb jindAbAd)
इन्किलाब जिन्दाबाद(inkilAb jindAbAd)
मुर्दाबाद (murdAbAd)
मुर्दाबाद(murdAbAd)
महंगाई मुर्दाबाद (mahaMgAI murdAbAd)
महागाई मुर्दाबाद(mahAgAI murdAbAd)
__का विजय हो (kA vijay ho)
---चा विजय असो(chA vijay aso)
भारतका विजय हो(bhAratakA vijay ho)
भारताचा विजय असो(bhAratAchA vijay aso)
कौशिकका विजय हो (kaushikakA vijay ho)
कौशिकचा विजय असो(kaushikachA vijay aso)
सप्रेम भेट (saprem bheT)
सप्रेम भेट(saprem bheT)
प्रिय (priya)
प्रिय(priya)
After someone gives you wishes; in English, we may respond as “Same to you”. In Marathi, we can say that तुला पण (tulA paN ) or तुला पण शुभेच्छा ( tulA paN shubhechChA ) which literally means “to you as well” and “wishes to you as well”.

title: Greeting,Wishes,Blessings,Slogans in Marathi
content: Greeting,Wishes,Blessings,Slogans in Marathi
intro: Common greetings, wishes, blessings, and slogans. Template for occasion: ___च्या हार्दिक शुभेच्छा. Same to you: तुला पण or तुला पण शुभेच्छा.

blocks.heading: Blessings
rows: My blessings are always with you
माझे आशिर्वाद सदैव तुझ्या पठीशी आहेत
mAjhe AshirvAd sadaiva tujhyA paThIshI Ahet

blocks.heading: Same to you
blocks.content: तुला पण (tulA paN) or तुला पण शुभेच्छा (tulA paN shubhechChA)

title: I Love you In Marathi. Proposing someone in Marathi
content: I Love you In Marathi. Proposing someone in Marathi
intro: How to say I love you in Marathi, how to propose. (Boy to Girl) / (Girl to Boy) = gender of speaker.

blocks.heading: Love & proposing sentences
rows: But my family members do not like it
पण माझ्या घरच्यांना हे पसंत नाही.
paN mAjhyA gharachyAMnA he pasaMt nAhI.
We shall elope and get married
No. I do not want to get married secretly
नाही. मला लपूनछपून लग्न करायचे नाही.
nAhI. malA lapUnaChapUn lagn karAyache nAhI.
I will marry only if my parents allow
माझ्या आई वडीलांनी परवानगी दिली तरच मी लग्न करीन
mAjhyA AI vaDIlAMnI paravAnagI dilI tarach mI lagn karIn
I am ready to leave my home, my parents for you
तुझ्यासाठी मी माझं घर, माझ्या आईवडिलांना सोडायला तयार आहे
tujhyAsAThI mI mAjhM ghar, mAjhyA AIvaDilAMnA soDAyalA tayAr Ahe
I am ready to come with you anywhere you say
तू म्हणशील तिथे मी तुझ्या बरोबर यायला तयार आहे
tU mhaNashIl tithe mI tujhyA barobar yAyalA tayAr Ahe
Why do you sulk (Boy to Girl)
Why do you sulk (Girl to Boy)
Why are you angry (Boy to Girl)
Why are you angry (Girl to Boy)
Why don't you talk with me?
I will not talk with you
I can not live without you
I want to marry your daughter
मला तुमच्या मुलीशी लग्न करायचे आहे
malA tumachyA mulIshI lagn karAyache Ahe
I want to marry your son
मला तुमच्या मुलाशी लग्न करायचे आहे
malA tumachyA mulAshI lagn karAyache Ahe
I will keep your son/daughter happy
मी तुमच्या मुलाला/मुलीला नेहमी सुखात/ आनंदात ठेवीन
mI tumachyA mulAlA/mulIlA nehamI sukhAt/ AnaMdAt ThevIn
We will always live very happily

title: I Love you In Marathi. Saying मै तुमसे प्यार करता हूं in Marathi
content:
Sentences related to Love and proposing some one.
This will answer general questions like “How to say I love you in Marathi”, “How to propose a girl in Marathi”.
Those sentences which will change as per gender of the speaker are mentioned accordingly e.g.(Boy to Girl) or (Girl to Boy). If nothing is mentioned then sentences are same irrespective of gender
Hindi
Marathi
मुझे तुम से मुहब्बत  है  (mujhe tum se muhabbat  hai )
माझं तुझ्यावर प्रेम आहे (mAjhaM tujhyAvar prem Ahe)
मै तुमसे प्यार करता हूं (mai tumase pyAr karatA hUM)
मी तुझ्यावर प्रेम करतो  (mI tujhyAvar prem karato )
मै तुमसे प्यार करती हूं (mai tumase pyAr karatI hUM)
मी तुझ्यावर प्रेम करते (mI tujhyAvar prem karate)
मुझे तुम पसंद हो (mujhe tum pasaMd ho)(Girl to Boy)
मला तू आवडतोस (malA tU AvaDatos)
मुझे तुम पसंद हो (mujhe tum pasaMd ho) (Boy to Girl)
मला तू आवडतेस (malA tU AvaDates)
तुम बहुत सुंदर हो (tum bahut suMdar ho)
तू खूप सुंदर आहेस ( tU khUp suMdar Ahes )
क्या हम शादी करें ? (kyA ham shAdI kareM ?)
आपण लग्न करूया का. (ApaN lagn karUyA kA.)
तुम मुझसे शादी करोगे/करोगी क्या (tum mujhase shAdI karoge/karogI kyA)
तू माझ्याशी लग्न करशील का ? (tU mAjhyAshI lagn karashIl kA ?)
हां. हम शादी करते है.  (hAM. ham shAdI karate hai. )
हो. आपण लग्न करूया. (ho. ApaN lagn karUyA.)
लेकिन मेरे घरवालोंको यह पसंद नही. (lekin mere gharavAloMko yah pasaMd nahI.)
पण माझ्या घरच्यांना हे पसंत नाही.  (paN mAjhyA gharachyAMnA he pasaMt nAhI. )
OR
पण माझ्या घरच्यांना हे आवडणार नाही.  (paN mAjhyA gharachyAMnA he AvaDaNAr nAhI. )
हम भागके शादी करेंगे (ham bhAgake shAdI kareMge)
आपण पळून जाऊन लग्न करू. (ApaN paLUn jAUn lagn karU.)
नही मुझे छुपछुपके शादी नहीं करनी   (nahI mujhe ChupaChupake shAdI nahIM karanI)
नाही. मला लपूनछपून लग्न करायचे नाही. (nAhI. malA lapUnaChapUn lagn karAyache nAhI.)
मेरे मा-पिताजी ने अनुमती दी तो ही मै शादी करुंगा/करुंगी (mere mA-pitAjI ne anumatI dI to hI mai shAdI karuMgA/karuMgI)
माझ्या आई वडीलांनी परवानगी दिली तरच मी लग्न करीन ( mAjhyA AI vaDIlAMnI paravAnagI dilI tarach mI lagn karIn )
मै तेरे मा-पिताजी को समझाऊंगा/समझाऊंगी  (mai tere mA-pitAjI ko samajhAUMgA/samajhAUMgI )
मी तुझ्या आई वडिलांना समजावेन.  (mI tujhyA AI vaDilAMnA samajAven. )
मै तुम्हारे लिये मेरा घर, मेरे मा-पिता को छोडने के लिये तैयार हूं (mai tumhAre liye merA ghar, mere mA-pitA ko ChoDane ke liye taiyAr hUM)
तुझ्यासाठी मी माझं घर, माझ्या आईवडिलांना सोडायला तयार आहे ( tujhyAsAThI mI mAjhM ghar, mAjhyA AIvaDilAMnA soDAyalA tayAr Ahe )
तुम जहां कहोगे वहां मै तुम्हारे साथ आने के लिये तैयार हूं (tum jahAM kahoge vahAM mai tumhAre sAth Ane ke liye taiyAr hUM)
तू म्हणशील तिथे मी तुझ्या बरोबर यायला तयार आहे ( tU mhaNashIl tithe mI tujhyA barobar yAyalA tayAr Ahe )
मेरा तुम पर पूरा भरोसा है (merA tuma par pUrA bharosA hai)
माझा तुझ्यावर पूर्ण विश्वास आहे (mAjhA tujhyAvar pUrN vishvAs Ahe)
तुम मुझसे क्यूं रूठी हो  (tum mujhase kyUM rUThI ho ) (Boy to Girl)
तू का रुसली आहेस (tU kA rusalI Ahes)
तुम मुझसे क्यूं रूठे हो  (tum mujhase kyUM rUThe ho )(Girl to Boy )
तू का रुसला आहेस (tU kA rusalA Ahes)
तुम क्यूं गुस्सा हो  (tum kyUM gussA ho )(Boy to Girl)
तू का रागावली आहेस (tU kA rAgAvalI Ahes)
तुम क्यूं गुस्सा हो  (tum kyUM gussA ho ) (Girl to Boy )
तू का रागावला  आहेस (tU kA rAgAvalA  Ahes)
तुम मुझसे बात क्यों नही करते  (tum mujhase bAt kyoM nahI karate )
तू माझ्याशी का बोलत नाहीस (tU mAjhyAshI kA bolat nAhIs)
मै तुमसे बात नहीं करूंगा/करूंगी (mai tumase bAt nahIM karUMgA/karUMgI)
मी तुझ्याशी बोलणार नाही (mI tujhyAshI bolaNAr nAhI)
मुझे तुम्हारी बहुत याद आती है (mujhe tumhArI bahut yAd AtI hai)
मला तुझी खूप आठवण येते ( malA tujhI khUp AThavaN yete )
मै तुम्हारे सिवा नहीं जी सकता/सकती (mai tumhAre sivA nahIM jI sakatA/sakatI)
मी तुझ्याशिवाय जगू शकत नाही (mI tujhyAshivAy jagU shakat nAhI )
मुझे तुम्हारे बेटी के साथ शादी करनी है (mujhe tumhAre beTI ke sAth shAdI karanI hai)
मला तुमच्या मुलीशी लग्न करायचे आहे  (malA tumachyA mulIshI lagn karAyache Ahe )
मुझे तुम्हारे बेटे के साथ शादी करनी है (mujhe tumhAre beTe ke sAth shAdI karanI hai)
मला तुमच्या मुलाशी लग्न करायचे आहे  (malA tumachyA mulAshI lagn karAyache Ahe )
मै तुम्हारे बेटेको/बेटीको हमेशा सुखी/खुश रखूंगा/रखूंगी (mai tumhAre beTeko/beTIko hameshA sukhI/khush rakhUMgA/rakhUMgI)
मी तुमच्या मुलाला/मुलीला नेहमी सुखात/ आनंदात ठेवीन ( mI tumachyA mulAlA/mulIlA nehamI sukhAt/ AnaMdAt ThevIn )
हम हमेशा सुखी/खुश रहेंगे (ham hameshA sukhI/khush raheMge)
आपण नेहमी सुखात/आनंदात राहू.(ApaN nehamI sukhAt/ AnaMdAt rAhU )
हम दोनो राजा-रानी (ham dono rAjA-rAnI)
आपण दोघं राजाराणी ( ApaN doghaM rAjArANI )
