title: Preposition "TO" in Marathi
content: Preposition "TO" in Marathi
intro: Preposition "To" is ला (lA) in Marathi. Special forms incorporate ला, so no need to add it again.

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
The preposition "To" in English is ला (lA) in Marathi. But "To" is handled differently: special forms are created incorporating ला. So no need to add ला again.

e.g. "to him" in English is त्याला (tyAlA) in Marathi. The form already has ला in it.

Give it to him -> हे त्याला दे (he tyAlA de)

blocks.heading: Self
blocks.content: For "self" (स्वतः): form with "To" is स्वतःला (svatHlA).

blocks.heading: Old style Marathi using स(sa)
blocks.content:
In old/formal Marathi, prefix स(sa) is used instead of ला(lA) for third person:
- To him/it: त्याला = त्यास (tyAs)
- To her: तिला = तीस (tIs)
- To them: त्यांना = त्यांस (tyAMs)
- To you (very formal): आपल्याला = आपणांस (ApanAs)

Examples: धुम्रपानास मनाई आहे (prohibition to smoking); सर्व पाहुण्यांस भेटवस्तू मिळेल (gift to all guests).

For first/second person (rarely used): To me = मज (maja), To you = तुज (tuja).

title: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings
content: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings
intro: Advanced topic. Some prepositions sound similar but have different meanings. Early learners can skip.

blocks.heading: 1: मध्ये (madhye) & मधून (madhUn)
blocks.content:
**मध्ये** (madhye) = inside. **मधून** (madhUn) = from inside.

Keep tea-spoon inside drawer, take table-spoon from drawer -> टि-स्पून ड्रॉवर मध्ये ठेव आणि टेबल स्पून ड्रॉवर मधून बाहेर काढ (Ti-spUn drawer madhye Thev ANi Tebal spUn drawer madhUn bAher kADh).

**मधला** (madhalA) = adjective (middle/one within). Middle stump -> मधला स्टंप (madhalA sTaMp).

blocks.heading: 2: कडे (kaDe) & कडून (kaDUn)
blocks.content:
**कडे** (kaDe) = to/towards. **कडून** (kaDUn) = from. कडून = source/direction; कडे = destination.

Throne passed from king to prince -> राजाच्या मृत्यूनंतर सिंहासन राजाकडून राजपुत्राकडे गेले (rAjAchyA mRutyUnaMtar siMhAsan rAjAkaDUn rAjaputrAkaDe gele).
Sun travels from east towards west -> सूर्य पूर्वेकडून पश्चिमेकडे प्रवास करतो (sUrya pUrvekaDUn paSchimekaDe pravAs karato).

blocks.heading: 3: पासून (pAsUn) & कडून (kaDUn)
blocks.content:
Both mean "from". **पासून** (pAsUn) = starting point (use with पर्यंत). **कडून** (kaDUn) = direction of movement (use with कडे).

Distance Mumbai to Pune -> मुंबईपासून पुण्यापर्यंतचे अंतर 149 KM आहे (muMbaIpAsUn puNyAparyaMtache aMtar 149 KM Ahe).
From his place to my place -> त्याच्याकडून माझ्याकडे यायला तीन तास लागतात (tyAchyAkaDUn mAjhyAkaDe yAyalA tIn tAs lAgatAt).

For time: always पासून-पर्यंत (pAsUn-paryaMt). कालपासून आजपर्यंत (kAlapAsUn AjaparyaMt). Never कालकडून (kAlakaDUn).

blocks.heading: 4: हून, तून, वरून, कडून
blocks.content:
**हून** (hUn): from a place (city, village). He came from London -> तो काल लंडनहून आला (to kAl laMDanahUn AlA).

**तून** (tUn): from inside. Similar to मधून. From village -> गावातून/गावामधून (gAvAtUn/gAvAmadhUn).

**वरून** (varUn): via. Train via your city -> तुझ्या शहरावरून जाईल (tujhyA shaharAvarUn jAIl). What did you bring from (via) Delhi? -> दिल्लीवरून काय आणलेस? (dillIvarUn kAy ANales?)

**कडून** (kaDUn): other cases. I did not get help from him -> मला त्याच्याकडून काही मदत मिळाली नाही (malA tyAchyAkaDUn kAhI madat miLAlI nAhI).

blocks.heading: 5: ला (lA) & other cases
blocks.content: **ला** (lA) = destination. To office -> ऑफिसला (ophisalA). To school -> शाळेला (shALelA) or शाळेत (shALet). To home -> घरी (gharI) only (not घरला in standard Marathi).

blocks.heading: 6: मधे, मध्ये, त
blocks.content:
All mean in/among. Interchangeable. Append to सामान्यरूप (sAmAnya rUp).

Pot (माठ mATh) -> माठामधे/माठामध्ये/माठात (mAThAmadhe/mAThAmadhye/mAThAt). Jar (बरणी baraNI) -> बरणीमधे/बरणीमध्ये/बरणीत (baraNImadhe/baraNImadhye/baraNIt). Leaf (पान pAn) -> पानामधे/पानामध्ये/पानात (pAnAmadhe/pAnAmadhye/pAnAt).

For English words: prefer मधे/मध्ये (madhe/madhye). त (t) with सामान्यरूप can sound odd for English words.

title: Prepositions with verbs in Marathi
content:
In Hindi, we use अव्यय (avyay) i.e. prepositions with verb e.g. बोलने के बाद ( bolane ke bAd ), बोलने के लिये (bolane ke liye), बोलते बोलते ( bolate bolate), बोलने में ( bolane meM ) etc.
As you can observe, in Hindi prepositions viz. बाद, लिये, में etc. are added to सामान्य रूप (sAmAnya rUp) of verb viz. “बोलने”
In Marathi also, सामान्य रूप (sAmAnya rUp) of the verb is used to append preposition.
In Marathi generally, ण्या (NyA) is suffixed to verb before adding अव्यय ( avyay).
e.g.
Verb in Hindi
vern in Marahti
सामान्य रूप (sAmAnya rUp) in Marathi of verb
कर (kar )
कर (kar )
करण्या karaNyA
बोल (bol )
बोल (bol )
बोलण्या (bolaNyA )
नाच (nAch )
नाच (nAch )
नाचण्या (nAchaNyA )
बोलने के बारेमें ( bolane ke bAremeM):- बोलण्याबद्दल (bolaNyAbaddal)
खाने के लिये ( khAne ke liye):- खाण्यासाठी (khANyAsAThI)
आने का कारन (Ane kA kAran):- येण्याचे कारण (yeNyAche kAraN)

title: Prepositions with Verbs
content: Prepositions with Verbs in Marathi
intro: When using a preposition with pronouns or nouns, the form changes which is called सामान्य रूप (sAmAnya rUp) in Marathi or Oblique case form in English. The same happens with verbs. In English we say 'after doing', 'before singing', 'while writing' using the '-ing' form. In Marathi ण्या (NyA) is suffixed to verb.

blocks.heading: Suffix चे (che) for past/soon action
blocks.content: When talking about past action or action about to happen soon; suffix चे(che) to verb form.

blocks.heading: Suffix चे (che) examples
rows: He told about his coming to school
तो शाळेत आल्याचे त्याने मला सांगितले
to shALet AlyAche tyAne malA sAMgitale
He told me that he is coming
तो शाळेत येत असल्याचे त्याने मला सांगितले
to shALet yet asalyAche tyAne malA sAMgitale
He told me that he is going to come
तो शाळेत येणार असल्याचे त्याने मला सांगितले
to shALet yeNAr asalyAche tyAne malA sAMgitale
I don't remember she having said something
ती असे काही बोलली असल्याचे मला आठवत नाही
tI ase kAhI bolalI asalyAche malA AThavat nAhI
I don't remember she having eaten mango
तीने आंबा खाल्ल्याचे मला आठवत नाही
tIne AMbA khAllyAche malA AThavat nAhI

blocks.heading: Special: preposition While
blocks.content: Suffix for 'while' is ताना (tAnA). Two ways: 1) Do not stop while I speak – verb in simple present. 2) Do not stop while I am speaking – verb in present continuous: अस(as) + ताना(tAnA) = असताना (asatAnA).

blocks.heading: While – present continuous (while I am speaking)
Special: preposition To
blocks.content: For 'Come to see this movie', 'Come to drink coffee' – we use आयला (AyalA) suffix.

blocks.heading: Formal/old style: ण्यास (NyAs)
blocks.content: For news, formal text or old style: verb root + ण्यास(NyAs).

blocks.heading: Formal style – sentence examples
rows: Court asked to be present tomorrow

blocks.heading: Special: preposition And
blocks.content: To join two verbs (one action followed by another): suffix ऊन (Un) to first verb.

blocks.heading: And – examples (ऊन suffix)
rows: They will go and check it

blocks.heading: Special: Till / Until
blocks.content: Till/Until in Marathi is पर्यंत (paryaMt). Suffix ए(e) to verb stem. If verb stem does not end with vowel अ(a) then suffix ई(I).

title: Present Continuous Tense in Marathi
content: Present Continuous Tense in Marathi
intro: Learn how to form Present Continuous tense: Pronoun + verb (Simple Present form) + form of असणे.

blocks.heading: Structure
blocks.content:
After learning Simple Present Tense of "To be" in Marathi, it is easy to march toward learning Present Continuous tense (PCT) in Marathi.

In English PCT sentence is formed as: Pronoun + form of To be + Verb+-ing (e.g. I am doing)

In Marathi PCT sentence is formed as: Pronoun + form of verb in Simple Present Tense + form of असणे (asaNe)

Example: I -> मी, Do -> कर, Am -> आहे. So मी करतो आहे (mI karato Ahe) = I am doing.

Another example: "He eats" is तो खातो. Form of असणे for He is आहे. So तो खातो आहे = He is eating.

Similarly: ती रडते -> ती रडते आहे (tI raDate Ahe) = She is crying.

blocks.heading: Exceptions
blocks.content:
**1) Exception for "You":** You (male) eat -> तू खातोस (tU khAtos). Form of असणे for You is आहेस (Ahes). So Present continuous would be "तू खातोस आहेस". But the ending स(s) is NOT used in the verb. The sentence is: **तू खातो आहेस**.

**2) Exception for "They":** They eat -> ते/त्या/ती खातात (te/tyA/tI khAtAt). Form of असणे for They is आहेत (Ahet). So it would be "ते/त्या/ती खातात आहेत". But the ending तात (tAt) is NOT used. Instead only त(t) is used. The sentence is: **ते/त्या/ती खात आहेत**.

blocks.heading: Suffix chart for Present Continuous (exceptions highlighted)
Style 2 of Present Continuous
blocks.content:
Second style is the same as above. The only difference is that for all nouns and pronouns the suffix for the verb is त(t). Both styles are correct and part of standard Marathi.

**Examples:**
- I (male) am doing: Style 1 मी करतो आहे | Style 2 मी करत आहे
- She is speaking: Style 1 ती बोलते आहे | Style 2 ती बोलत आहे
- It is moving: Style 1 ते हलते आहे | Style 2 ते हलत आहे
- You (male) are crying: Style 1 तू रडतो आहेस | Style 2 तू रडत आहेस

Learning these rules will help you study the rules for other continuous tenses.

blocks.heading: Listen
blocks.content: Listen examples of Style 1 and Style 2 online.

title: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Marathi
content: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Marathi
intro: Perfect tenses = Simple past sentence + form of असणे. If verb has object, use form as per object; otherwise as per subject.

blocks.heading: Rule 1: Verbs with noun/object
blocks.content:
**Logic:** Simple past sentence + form of असणे as per gender/multiplicity of **noun** and tense.

**Example:** "I have/had/will have opened box"
- Simple past: मी खोका उघडला (mI khokA ughaDalA). Object = Box (masculine).
- Past perfect: + होता -> मी खोका उघडला होता
- Present perfect: + आहे -> मी खोका उघडला आहे
- Future perfect: + असेल -> मी खोका उघडला असेल

blocks.heading: Rule 2: Verbs without noun/object
blocks.content:
**Logic:** Simple past sentence + form of असणे as per gender/multiplicity of **Subject** and tense.

**Example:** "I(male) have/had/will have spoken"
- Simple past: मी बोललो (mI bolalo). Subject = I(male).
- Past perfect: + होतो -> मी बोललो होतो
- Present perfect: + आहे -> मी बोललो आहे
- Future perfect: + असेन -> मी बोललो असेन

blocks.heading: Forms of असणे for all three tenses
Example 1: She has/had/will have opened door
blocks.content: Simple past: तीने दरवाजा उघडला (tIne daravAjA ughaDalA). Object = door (masculine). Append form of असणे for He.

blocks.heading: Example 2: You(female) have/had/will have danced
blocks.content: Simple past: तू नाचलीस (tU nAchalIs). Verb has no object, so use form as per subject. Omit स from नाचलीस for Perfect.

blocks.heading: Example 3: You(plural) have/had/will have danced
blocks.content: Simple past: तुम्ही नाचलात (tumhI nAchalAt). Omit त from नाचलात for Perfect.

blocks.heading: Note
blocks.content: For तू and तुम्ही, omit ending स(s) and त(t) of simple past when making Perfect tense.

title: Pronouns and Articles in Marathi
content: Pronouns and Articles in Marathi
intro: Learn Marathi pronouns and the difference from English articles. Use the speaker buttons to hear pronunciation.

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
Let us learn Marathi's pronouns today. The below chart has the list.

As I would always say, go through the list once. But do not get daunted by the details. When we will start preparing sentences, come back to the chart and get the required pronoun. Using them frequently will make you familiar. And afterward, you will not need to come back to this chart.

blocks.heading: Difference between आम्ही (AmhI) and आपण (ApaN)
blocks.content: **आम्ही (AmhI)** is used when the listener is not included. **आपण (ApaN)** is used when the listener is included.

blocks.heading: आम्ही vs आपण – Examples
rows: We went to the party (listener not included)
We went to the party (listener included)

blocks.heading: Learn Articles in Marathi and English
blocks.content: In English there are three articles: "a", "an", and "the". In Marathi there is no custom of using such articles.

title: Pronunciation of Anusvar अनुस्वार
content: Pronunciation of Anusvar अनुस्वार
intro: The dot (ं) on a character is called अनुस्वार (anusvAr). Its pronunciation depends on the character that follows it. Use the speaker buttons to hear examples.

blocks.heading: Explanation for अनुस्वार (anusvAr)
blocks.content:
In the table for vowels there is row:

अं  aM  — As sound of "um" in number, sound of "um" as in jump

This will need more attention to understand its pronunciation. The dot given on the character is called **अनुस्वार** (anusvAr).

The anusvaar (अनुस्वार) is a nasal sound. The अनुस्वार itself does not determine the pronunciation of a word. Rather, it is the character that **follows** the अनुस्वार that determines the pronunciation. There are several possibilities that affect the pronunciation of अनुस्वार.

So though we will use capital 'M' to indicate अनुस्वार, you may need to pronounce it differently depending on the case.

blocks.heading: Rule 1: Followed by क, ख, ग, घ
blocks.content: If the nasal dot is followed by क (k), ख (kh), ग (g), घ (gh), the nasal dot is pronounced as ङ (ṅ) — e.g. 'n' in "sing".

blocks.heading: Rule 2: Followed by च, छ, ज, झ
blocks.content: If the nasal dot is followed by च (ch), छ (Ch), ज (j), झ (jh), the dot is pronounced as ञ (ñ) — e.g. 'n' in the English word "munch".

blocks.heading: Rule 3: Followed by ट, ठ, ड, ढ, ण
blocks.content: If the nasal dot is followed by ट (T), ठ (Th), ड (D), ढ (Dh), ण (N), the dot is pronounced as ण (N).

blocks.heading: Rule 4: Followed by त, थ, द, ध, न
blocks.content: If the nasal dot is followed by त (t), थ (th), द (d), ध (dh), न (n), the dot is pronounced as न (n).

blocks.heading: Rule 5: Followed by प, फ, ब, भ, म
blocks.content: If the nasal dot is followed by प (p), फ (ph), ब (b), भ (bh), म (m), the dot is pronounced as म (m).

blocks.heading: Rule 6: Followed by य, र, ल, व, श, ष, स, ह, ळ, क्ष, ज्ञ
blocks.content: If the nasal dot is followed by य (y), र (r), ल (l), व (v), श (sh), ष (Sh), स (s), ह (h), ळ (L), क्ष (kSh), ज्ञ (dny), the dot is pronounced as अं (aM).

blocks.heading: Rule 7: Followed by space
blocks.content:
If the nasal dot is followed by the space character, the dot is pronounced as half म (m).

Example: एवं = एवम् (evam)

blocks.heading: Rule 8: Colloquial change in pronunciation
blocks.content:
In day-to-day communication, words ending with vowel ए (e) are said a little differently. Instead of pronouncing ए (e), the consonant is pronounced elongated. This change is indicated by अनुस्वार; though there is no nasal sound. (To know more, refer **Short forms in spoken Marathi**)

E.g. "Trees" in English is झाडे (jhADe) in Marathi. But in day-to-day communication, it can be said as झाडं (jhADM), where ending ड (d) is pronounced elongated. Similarly "Leaves" is पाने (pAne). It can also be said as पानं (pAnM).

**Rule of thumb for combined words:**
- If the word ends with तर (tar), तरी (tarI), ही (hI) then it is a combined word. e.g. कसंतरी (kasaMtarI), कुठंही (kuThMhI), खरंतर (kharaMtar)
- If the word is a pair of two rhyming words, it is a combined word. e.g. झाडं-बिडं (jhADMbiDM), हलकं-फुलकं (halakaMphulakaM), साधं-बिधं (sAdhaMbidhaM)
- If a word is a pair of opposite words, it is a combined word. e.g. खरं-खोटं (kharaM-khoTM), भलं-बुरं (bhalM-buraM)

So you should know if it is a combined word or a single word to pronounce it correctly.

blocks.heading: Rule 9: अनुस्वार in सामान्यरूप form of plural
blocks.content:
In सामान्यरूप form of plural of noun, whenever there is अनुस्वार at end, it is pronounced as light half न (n) irrespective of the following consonant.

**Example:** Word संप (saMp) → सामान्यरूप plural: संपां (saMpAM)
Full word: संपांपासून (saMpAMpAsUn) is pronounced as सम्पान्पासून (sampAnpAsUn).

Here the first अनुस्वार (in सं) is pronounced as half म (m) because next consonant is प (p) — Rule 5. But the second अनुस्वार (in पां) is pronounced as half न (n) even if next consonant is again प (p) — Rule 9.

**More examples:**
- माठ (mATh) → माठां (mAThAM) → माठांकरिता pronounced as माठान्करिता (mAThAnkaritA)
- पान (pAn) → पानां (pAnAM) → पानांकरिता pronounced as पानान्करिता (pAnAnkaritA)

title: Pronunciation of Anusvar अनुस्वार
content:
In the table for vowels there is row :
अं
aM
As sound of "um" in number, sound of "um" as in jum
This will need more attention to understand its pronunciation.
The dot given on the character is called “अनुस्वार” (anusvAr)
The anusvaar (अनुस्वार ) is a nasal sound. The अनुस्वार itself does not determine the pronunciation of a word. Rather, it is the character that follows the अनुस्वारthat determines the pronunciation. There are seven possibilities that affect the pronunciation of a अनुस्वार.
So though we will use capital ‘M’ to indicate अनुस्वार, you may need to pronounce it differently depending on the case.

title: Relations in Marathi
content: Relations in Marathi
intro: Family relations in Marathi. In-laws: सासर (in-laws' house), माहेर (girl's parents' house). भाचा-पुतण्या: man calls brother's son पुतण्या; lady calls brother's son भाचा.

blocks.heading: Note: भाचा-पुतण्या
blocks.content: Man: brother's son = पुतण्या, brother's daughter = पुतणी. Lady: brother's son = भाचा, brother's daughter = भाची. Both: sister's son = भाचा, sister's daughter = भाची.

title: Requesting-someone-"May-I","Can-you" etc.
content: Requesting May-I Can-you etc.
intro: May I: subject (मी/आम्ही) + verb+ऊ + का. May you/he: use Can format. Also: Do you give pen? style.

blocks.heading: May you / May he – use Can

title: samajhanA patA chalanA समझना पता चलना
content:
In Hindi, we use verb
समझना ( samajhanA )
which in Marathi is
समजणे (samajaNe )
The sentence structure in Marathi and Hindi is the same.
i.e. verb form is created as per tense and gender, multiplicity of object
मुझे इसका महत्त्व समझता है ( mujhe isakA mahattv samajhatA hai )
मला याचे महत्व समजते. (malA yAche mahatva samajate)
उसे तत्त्व समझा ( use tattv samajhA )
त्याला तत्त्व समजले ( tyAlA tattva samajale )
उन्हे उनकी भूल समझमे आयेगी ( unhe unakI bhUl samajhame AyegI )
त्यांना त्यांची चूक कळेल ( tyAMnA tyAMchI chUk kaLel )
Other phrase related to this concept is
पता चलना ( patA chalanA )
. In Marathi we use verb
कळणे ( kaLaNe )
or
समजणे (samajaNe )
. Sentece structure in Marathi is same as in Hindi.
तुम्हे पता चला क्या ( tumhe patA chalA kyA )
तुम्हाला कळले/समजले का ( tumhAlA kaLale/samajale kA )
उसे पता चलेगा ( use patA chalegA )
त्याला कळेल/समजेल ( tyAlA kaLel/samajel )
मुझे पता नही था ( mujhe patA nahI thA )
मला कळले/समजले नव्हते ( malA kaLale/samajale navhate )

title: Saying have to-करना पडेगा-जाना पडेगा-karana paDegA jAnA paDegA etc.
content:
Whenever we want to indicate that another person has to do some action, in Hindi we say, तुझे जाना पडेगा (tujhe jAnA paDegA),उसे दवाई लेनी पडेगी ( use davAI lenI paDegI), सलीम तुझे बदलना होगा (salIm tujhe badalanA hogA).
In Marathi, sentences structure is similar to Hindi. And it is formed using verb लागणे (“lAgaNe”) as follows:
Form of noun/pronoun
with preposition "To"
e.g. “to me”,”to him” etc.
verb+ “आयला “ (AyalA) OR
Verb+ form of “आवे"(Ave) as per object
form of लागणे
(“lAgaNe”)
as per tense and gender & multiplicity of object
त्याला
जायला / जावे
लागेल
By “form of आवे (Ave) as per gender” we mean depending on gender and multiplicity of the object the suffix will change as per
आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule
आवा-आवी-आवे-आवे-आव्या-आवी (AvA-AvI-Ave-Ave-AvyA-AvI)
e.g. for verb जाणे(jANe) form will be
Masculine singular जावा (jAvA)               Masculine plural जावे (jAve)
Feminine singular जावी (
jAvI
)                  Feminine plural जाव्या (
jAvyA
)
Neuter singular जावे (jAve)                       Neuter plural जावी (
jAvI
)
For अकर्मक(akarmak) verbs, i.e. which do not need a noun, always Neuter singular suffix आवे (Ave) is used
Take the below examples to clarify the concept:

title: Saying My/His/Her in Marathi
content: Saying My/His/Her in Marathi
intro: Forms change by gender of the object. Singular: चा/ची/चे. Plural: चे/च्या/ची. For proper nouns append to सामान्यरूप.

blocks.heading: My/His/Her etc.
blocks.content:
In English "My" is gender-neutral. In Marathi, form changes by gender of the **object**.

**My:** माझा (mAjhA) (m.) | माझी (mAjhI) (f.) | माझे (mAjhe) (n.) | Plural: माझे/माझ्या/माझी (mAjhe/mAjhyA/mAjhI)
- My son -> माझा मुलगा (mAjhA mulagA)
- My daughter -> माझी मुलगी (mAjhI mulagI)
- My Table -> माझे टेबल (mAjhe Tebal)
- My sons -> माझे मुलगे (mAjhe mulage) | My daughters -> माझ्या मुली (mAjhyA mulI) | My Tables -> माझी टेबले (mAjhI Tebale)

**For proper names:** Append चा/ची/चे (chA/chI/che) (singular) or चे/च्या/ची (che/chyA/chI) (plural) to the noun.
- Peter's son -> पीटरचा मुलगा (pITarachA mulagA) | Peter's daughter -> पीटरची मुलगी (pITarachI mulagI) | Peter's Table -> पीटरचे टेबल (pITarache Tebal)

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: ह्यांची झाडे (hyAMchI jhADe) (Tree is neuter plural)

blocks.heading: Suffixes with common nouns
blocks.content:
For common nouns, append चा/ची/चे (chA/chI/che) to **सामान्यरूप** (sAmAnya rUp) of the noun.
- Cuckoo -> कोकिळा (kokiLA). सामान्यरूप कोकिळे (kokiLe). Cuckoo's voice -> कोकिळेचा आवाज (kokiLechA AvAj)
- Name -> नाव (nAv). सामान्यरूप नावा (nAvA). Spelling of name -> नावाचे स्पेलिंग (nAvAche speliMg)
- Egg -> अंडे (aMDe). Price of egg -> अंड्याची किंमत (aMDyAchI kiMmat)

blocks.heading: Relating multiple words
blocks.content:
In a chain, use सामान्यरूप (sAmAnya rUp) for every word. Pronoun तो/ती/ते (to/tI/te) -> त्या (tyA). Suffix चा/ची/चे (chA/chI/che) -> च्या (chyA).

Example: Voice of that cuckoo -> त्या कोकिळेचा आवाज (tyA kokiLechA AvAj)
Beauty of voice of that cuckoo -> त्या कोकिळेच्या आवाजाचे सौंदर्य (tyA kokiLechyA AvAjache sauMdary)
Lyrics of song of narration... -> त्या कोकिळेच्या आवाजाच्या सौंदर्याच्या वर्णनाच्या गाण्याचे बोल (tyA kokiLechyA AvAjachyA sauMdaryAchyA varNanAchyA gANyAche bol)

title: Saying pasaMd hai पसंद है in Marathi
content:
In Hindi we say “मुझे आम पसंद है” (mujhe Am pasaMd hai), “उसे केक पसंद है(use kek pasaMd hai)”, “उन्हे कुत्ते पसंद थे ( unhe kutte pasMd the)
As we can see the verb “पसंद होना” ( pasaMd honA ) is formed as per the noun. And 2nd विभक्ती (vibhaktI ) is used to indicate who likes.
e.g. मुझे/इसे/उसे/हमे/उन्हे आम पसंद है
In Marathi same rules are followed. The verb is आवडणे ( AvaDaNe ).
मुझे आम पसंद है (mujhe Am pasaMd hai)
मला आंबा आवडतो (malA AMbA AvaDato )
उसे मॅग्गी पसंद है ( use maggI pasaMd hai )
त्याला मॅग्गी आवडते (tyAlA maggI AvaDate )
उन्हे कार्यक्रम पसंद आया ( unhe kAryakram pasaMd AyA )
त्यांना कार्यक्रम आवडला (tyAMnA kAryakram AvaDalA)
उन्हे खाना पसंद आयेगा ( unhe khAnA pasaMd AyegA )
त्यांना जेवण आवडेल (tyAMnA jevaN AvaDel)
उसे कुत्ते पसंद आये ( use kutte pasaMd Aye )
तीला कुत्रे आवडले ( tIlA kutre AvaDale )
उसे कुत्ते पसंद थे ( use kutte pasaMd the )
तीला कुत्रे आवडायचे ( tIlA kutre AvaDAyache )

title: Saying tum aap in Marathi तू-तुम-आप तू-तुम्ही
content:
आप (Ap) or तुम (tum) in Hindi is तुम्ही (tumhI) or आपण (ApaN) in Marathi.
“तू” in Hindi is “तू” in Marathi as well.
But do understand the difference in their usage especially its social background.
In Hindi, by default you address anyone else with respect i.e call him “आप” or “तुम”. And only when you do not want to show respect or show a very friendly relation you will address that person as “तू”.
In Marathi, it is opposite. By default, you will address anyone with “तू”. It does not show disrespect at all. It is natural for a native Marathi speaker to address son, daughter, brother, sister, cousin, mother, grandmother, unknown person of same age etc. with “तू”.
So when you translate a sentence from Hindi to Marathi, do NOT always translate “आप” or “तुम” with “तुम्ही”. It might sound unnatural in Marathi. So think, how closely you are related with the person in front of you and then better to go for “तू”.
That is why in this blog, I have given examples using “तू” in Hindi. So a sentence using “आप” or “तुम” should be the first thought in Hindi using “तू” and then go for Marathi translation.
Only when you have to show respect explicitly, you will use “तुम्ही”. So generally father, grandfather, teacher, boss, unknown person, an elder person are addressed with “तुम्ही”.
At home/in family relationships whom to call तू/तुम्ही is decided by tradition of that family and it is personal choice. Nowadays in many families (including mine) children address their mother & father as “तू”.
Similarly, few Hindi people use “हम” instead of “मै”. e.g. instead of “मुझे चाहिये”,”मुझे जाना है” they will say “हमे चाहिये”, “हमे जाना है”.
“हम” in Hindi is “आम्ही” in Marathi.
“मै” in Hindi is “मी ” in Marathi.
But while speaking Marathi NEVER use “आम्ही” instead of “मी”. People will laugh at you. They will think you as boasting yourself.
In old times kings, landlords or very respected person, elder persons etc. used to call themselves as “आम्ही” instead of “मी”, like in Hindi. But nowadays nobody follows this practice. So it will look very odd. So NEVER use it. When talking about yourself use “मी”.

title: Sentence using “Going to” phrase
content: Sentence using Going to phrase in Marathi
intro: In English, future tense sentences can be formed using the **'going to'** phrase. For example, **'He will come?'** can also be said as **'He is going to come'**. In Marathi, this sentence is formed using Subject + verb+णार + form of असणे (asaNe) in present tense.

blocks.heading: Marathi sentence structure
headers: Form of असणे (asaNe) in present tense

blocks.heading: Question form
blocks.content: Questions are formed by adding **का (kA)** at the end.

blocks.heading: Question structure
headers: Form of असणे (asaNe) in present tense

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: She is going to open door
Is she going to open door?

title: Sentence using "If-Then"
content: Sentence using If-Then in Marathi
intro: To specify the condition and its effect we use the 'If-then' structure in English. e.g. If you had gone then I would have gone as well. Had he eaten well then he would have become healthier. For If-then in Marathi, we use जर-तर (jar-tar). The past perfect tense is used as a base.

blocks.heading: Examples explained
rows: If he had opened the door then

blocks.heading: Note on verb forms
blocks.content: For verbs with object: verb and असणे form depend on gender/multiplicity of object. e.g. door is neuter-singular so we used उघडले असते. For window (feminine): त्याने खिडकी उघडली असती तर. For verbs without object: verb depends on doer. If he had spoken -> तो बोलला असता तर

blocks.heading: Full If-Then examples
rows: If you had spoken then she would have spoken
जर तू बोलला असतास तर ती बोलली असती
jar tU bolalA asatAs tar tI bolalI asatI
If he had cried then they would have ran
जर तो रडला असता तर ते धावले असते
jar to raDalA asatA tar te dhavale asate

blocks.heading: If-Then for future prediction
blocks.content: Structure can predict future: effect if some action is taken. e.g. If you go I will cry. In Marathi: condition part in simple past, effect part in future tense. Structure: जर condition (simple past) तर effect (future tense)

blocks.heading: Future prediction examples
rows: If you go I will cry
जर तू गेलास तर मी रडेन
jar tU gelAs tar mI raDen
If he sings she will dance

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase - old style
content: Sentence using Used to phrase – old style in Marathi
intro: In previous lesson 'Sentence using Used to phrase' we have seen 'Used to' sentences in Marathi. In this lesson we will see same sentences in old style. You will find elderly people using this style. It is less used by new generation. So you need not use it while speaking but you should understand it if you come across such sentences.

blocks.heading: Old style 1
blocks.content: Sentence structure: Pronoun + verb+त(t) + form of असणे(asaNe) i.e. असे/असू/असत (ase/asu/asat). For singular pronouns/nouns form is असे (ase). For plural pronouns/nouns form is असत (asat). Exception: for 'we' the form is असू (asu).

blocks.heading: Old style 1 examples
rows: Kaushik, Guru, sundar used to meet every day
My friends and I used to play cricket
माझे मित्र आणि मी क्रिकेट खेळत असू
mAjhe mitr ANi mI krikeT kheLat asU

blocks.heading: Note on असत (asat)
blocks.content: असत(asat) is sometimes dropped from sentences with third person plural. e.g. कौशिक,गुरू,सुंदर रोज भेटत (kaushik,gurU,suMdar roj bheTat). They used to watch the news every day -> ते रोज बातम्या बघत (te roj bAtamyA baghat)

blocks.heading: Old style 2
blocks.content: Another older style for first and third-person singular: append ई(I) to the verb. For verbs ending with sound अ(a) instead of ई(I) ए(e) can also be appended.

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase
content: Sentence using Used to phrase in Marathi
intro: In English we use the phrase 'used to' to indicate repetitive events. e.g. I used to go to morning walk when I was in school. He used to play bad before he got the coaching. Similar structure in Marathi: Subject + verb+suffix

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: They (plural of he) used to eat mangos
They (plural of she) used to play cricket

blocks.heading: Negative sentences
blocks.content: Negative sentences can be formed by adding नाही (nAhI) after verb form. Depending on subject नाही/नाहीस/नाहीत (nAhI/nAhIs/nAhIt) is added. Refer Negative Sentences – Present tense in Marathi.

blocks.heading: Negative examples
rows: I did not use to do
She did not use to speak
You did not use to dance
They (plural of he) did not use to eat mangos
She did not use to be quiet

blocks.heading: Negative for असणे (asaNe)
blocks.content: For 'to be' i.e. असणे (asaNe) a negative form can be created by prefixing न(n). i.e. न(n) + असायचा (asAyachA) -> नसायचा (nasAyachA). न(n) + असायची(asAyachI) -> नसायची (nasAyachI)

blocks.heading: Negative असणे examples
rows: He did not use to be there
She did not use to be quiet

blocks.heading: Present tense of used to
blocks.content: In English the present tense of 'used to' conveys different meaning – familiarity of action. e.g. I am used to lifting heavy weights. Here 'am used to' indicates that action is familiar/easier to me. So in English we do not have any parallel phrase. 'used to' says event occurred frequently in past. The present tense form in Marathi will indicate that event occurs frequently in present.

blocks.heading: Present tense – असणे forms by pronoun
Present tense examples
rows: Those girls are used to laugh
I am used to be there
She is used to be quiet

blocks.heading: Present tense negative
blocks.content: For 'to be' i.e. असणे (asaNe) negative form: prefix न(n). न(n) + असतात(asatAt) -> नसतात (nasatAt). न(n) + असते(asate) -> नसते (nasate)

blocks.heading: Present tense negative examples
rows: He is not used to dance
She is not used to speak

title: Sentences using "If-Then" Style 2
content: Sentences using If-Then Style 2
intro: Formal style: change verb suffix to ल्यास(lyAs). Past tense base: बोलला → बोलल्यास (If he speaks). Negative: न बोलल्यास.

blocks.heading: Structure
blocks.content: Use past tense as base. Change verb suffix to ल्यास(lyAs). e.g. He spoke तो बोलला → If he speaks तो बोलल्यास.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: तिने खोका उघडल्यास (If she opens box)
त्यांनी आंबा खाल्ल्यास (If they eat mango)

blocks.heading: Real examples
rows: If employee works 1 year, gratuity right
१ वर्ष काम केल्यास कर्मचाऱ्याला Gratuityचा अधिकार मिळेल
1 varSh kAm kelyAs karmachAryAlA GratuitychA adhikAr miLel
If wrong info given, disciplinary action
चुकीची माहिती दिल्यास शिस्तभंगाची कारवाई होईल
chukIchI mAhitI dilyAs shistabhMgAchI kAravAI hoIl
If possible, come to meet me

title: Sentences using "would" in Marathi
content: Sentences using would
intro: No exact replacement for 'would' in Marathi. Use: past→say as 'will'; unreal→as if-then without जर-तर; politeness→use 'will' instead.

blocks.heading: To talk about the past
blocks.content: In reported speech, say as if using 'will' instead of 'would'.

blocks.heading: Past/reported speech
rows: Kaushik said that he would eat
I thought I would be late so I would have to run
मला वाटले मला उशीर होईल म्हणून मला धावावे लागेल
malA vATale malA ushIr hoIl mhaNUn malA dhAvAve lAgel

blocks.heading: Unreal situations
blocks.content: Say as if-then but omit जर-तर. Use 'would have' form.

blocks.heading: Unreal examples
rows: I would love to visit New York
मला न्यूयॉर्कला भेट द्यायला आवडली असती
malA nyUy~orkalA bheT dyAyalA AvaDalI asatI
We would go, but we are too busy
आपण गेलो असतो, पण आपण खूप व्यग्र आहोत
ApaN gelo asato, paN ApaN khUp vyagr Ahot
I would not disclose it in any condition
मी कुठल्याही परिस्थितीत उघड केले नसते
mI kuThalyAhI paristhitIt ughaD kele nasate

blocks.heading: For politeness
blocks.content: Use 'will' instead of 'would'.

blocks.heading: Politeness examples
rows: Would you like to drink tea?
I would suggest you to take this medicine
मी हे औषध घेण्याचा सल्ला तुम्हाला देईन
mI he auShadh gheNyAchA sallA tumhAlA deIn

title: Sentences with person or living things as object
content: Sentences with person or living things as object
intro: When the object is a person or living thing, use Preposition "To" (ला/ना) with the object. In simple past and perfect tenses, verb form is always neuter-singular.

blocks.heading: Point 1: Use Preposition To
blocks.content:
For inanimate objects: I see dog -> मी कुत्रा बघतो (mI kutrA baghato). For a person (Kaushik): मी कौशिक बघतो (mI kaushik baghato) is grammatically correct but not natural. When talking about a person, use **ला** (lA) with singular/**ना** (nA) with plural or the corresponding pronoun form.

Like English "I see him" not "I see he":
- I see Kaushik -> मी कौशिकला बघतो (mI kaushikalA baghato)
- I see him -> मी त्याला बघतो (mI tyAlA baghato)
- He sees me -> तो मला बघतो (to malA baghato)
- He is shaking her -> तो तीला हलवतो आहे (to tIlA halavato Ahe)

blocks.heading: Point 2a: Simple past - verb always neuter-singular
blocks.content:
For person/living object, verb form is **always neuter-singular** regardless of object gender/plural:
- I saw Kaushik -> मी कौशिकला बघितले (mI kaushikalA baghitale) (not बघितला)
- I saw her -> मी तीला बघितले (mI tIlA baghitale)
- He shook students -> त्याने विद्यार्थ्यांना हलवले (tyAne vidyArthyAMnA halavale)

blocks.heading: Point 2b: Perfect tenses - same rule
blocks.content:
Same rule for Perfect tenses:
- I have seen Kaushik -> मी कौशिकला बघितले आहे (mI kaushikalA baghitale Ahe)
- I had seen her -> मी तीला बघितले होते (mI tIlA baghitale hote)
- He will have shaken students -> त्याने विद्यार्थ्यांना हलवलेले असेल (tyAne vidyArthyAMnA halavalele asel)

blocks.heading: He shakes friends - all tenses
rows: तो मित्रांना हलवत आहे (to mitrAMnA halavat Ahe)
त्याने मित्रांना हलवले आहे (tyAne mitrAMnA halavale Ahe)
तो मित्रांना हलवत होता (to mitrAMnA halavat hotA)
त्याने मित्रांना हलवले होते (tyAne mitrAMnA halavale hote)
तो मित्रांना हलवत असेल (to mitrAMnA halavat asel)
त्याने मित्रांना हलवलेले असेल (tyAne mitrAMnA halavalele asel)

blocks.heading: You push me - all tenses
rows: तू मला ढकलत आहेस (tU malA Dhakalat Ahes)
तू मला ढकलले आहेस (tU malA Dhakalale Ahes)
तू मला ढकलत होतास (tU malA Dhakalat hotAs)
तू मला ढकलले होते (tU malA Dhakalale hote)
तू मला ढकलत असशील (tU malA Dhakalat asashIl)
तू मला ढकललेले असेल (tU malA Dhakalalele asel)

title: Short forms in spoken Marathi मराठी बोली भाषा का प्रयोग marAThI bolI bhAShA kA prayog
content:
While speaking any language the pronunciations can be slightly different than bookish/written language. We tend to use the short form of verbs or words.
e.g. in English
He will – He’ ll
I had – I’d etc.
The short forms change as per the region, community and educational level of the speaker. There are many dialects i.e बोली भाषाएं in Marathi.
Here we are considering short forms used in the spoken language of standard Marathi.

title: Short forms in spoken Marathi
content: Short forms in spoken Marathi
intro: Spoken Marathi uses short forms: (1) आहे→य suffix in present continuous; (2) neuter ए→अं; (3) neuter verb ending ए→अं.

blocks.heading: Rule 1: Present continuous
blocks.content: आहे/आहेस/आहोत/आहात merged with verb as य suffix.

blocks.heading: Rule 2: Neuter ए→अं
blocks.content: Neuter words ending ए pronounced as अं. पाने→पानं, झाडे→झाडं.

blocks.heading: Rule 3: Neuter verb ending
blocks.content: When verb agrees with neuter subject/object, ए→अं. हलते→हलतं.

title: Showing uncertainty using May, Might
content: Showing uncertainty using May, Might
intro: Marathi uses simple future + कदाचित, एखादवेळेस, बहुतेक. For past uncertainty: future perfect + uncertainty word, or असावा form.

blocks.heading: Past uncertainty – future perfect
rows: बहुतेक ते काल परत गेले असतील
bahutek te kAla parat gele asatIl

title: Simple Future Tense in Marathi
content: Simple Future Tense in Marathi
intro: Similar to Simple Present, the structure is: Pronoun + verb + suffix.

blocks.heading: Structure
blocks.content:
Similar to Simple present tense, the Simple future tense also has the same sentence structure: **Pronoun followed by verb + suffix**.

**Example 1:** For I(male/female) the suffix is एन (en). चाल(chAl) + एन = चालेन (chAlen). "I will walk" -> मी चालेन (mI chAlen)

**Example 2:** For "We" the suffix is ऊ (U). चाल(chAl) + ऊ = चालू (chAlU). "We will walk" -> आम्ही चालू (AmhI chAlU)

**Example 3:** For "They" the suffix is तील (tIl). चाल(chAl) + तील = चालतील (chAlatIl). "They will walk" -> ते चालतील (te chAlatIl)

The form of "to Be" i.e. असणे (asaNe) also follows the same rule. So they are not irregular as in Simple tense.

I will be -> मी अस+एन -> मी असेन
We will be -> आम्ही अस+ऊ -> आम्ही असू
They will be -> ते/त्या/ती अस+तील -> ते/त्या/ती असतील

blocks.heading: Suffix and असणे forms for Simple Future
Explanation: Multiple suffixes
blocks.content:
**1) For "I":** a) Verbs ending with अ(a): both एन and ईन can be used. बोलेन & बोलीन, करेन & करीन are correct. b) Verbs NOT ending with अ(a): only ईन. देईन, होईन are correct; देएन, होएन are incorrect.

**2) For तुम्ही (You plural):** a) Verbs ending with अ(a): only आल. बोलाल, कराल. b) Verbs ending with आ(A): आल but no repetition. जा+आल=जाल (not जाआल). c) Verbs not ending with अ/आ: only याल. दे->द्याल, पि->प्याल. हो is exceptional: व्हाल.

**3) For He/She/It:** a) Verbs ending with अ(a): only एल. बोलेल, करेल. b) Verbs NOT ending with अ: only ईल. देईल, होईल.

**4) Verbs ending with उ(u):** Add व(v) before suffix. धु+व+एन->धुवेन. There are hardly any such verbs.

title: Simple Marathi conversation : A friend talking with other about his football match
content: Simple Marathi conversation : A friend talking with other about his football match

rows: There was your football match yesterday, wasn't it?
तुझी आज फुटबॉलची मॅच होती ना
tujhI Aj football m~ach hotI nA?
Is it a question? It's we!
एकच गोल झाला. आम्ही एक-शून्य असे जिंकलो.
ekach gol jhAlA. AmhI ekshUny ase jiMkalo.
नाही रे. मी तर डिफेंडर आहे.
nAhI re. mI tar DipheMDar Ahe.
Did it rain during your match?

tumachyA m~achachyA veLI pAUs paDalA kA?
Do inform me for next match
पुढच्या वेळी तुझी मॅच असेल तेव्हा मला आधी सांग.
puDhachyA veLI tujhI m~ach asel tevhA malA AdhI sAMg.

content: Simple Marathi conversation about food, lunch, meal

मी आज माझ्या मित्राच्या घरी गेले.
How important is food in your family?

tujhyA kuTuMbAchyAjIvanAt jevaN tayAr karaNe kitI mahatvAche Ahe?

somavArI AmhI uttar bhAratIy bhojan khAlle.
What did you cook this morning?

I prepared capsicum with gram flour

mI khUp chaviShT DhabbU mirachIche besan kele.

content: Simple Marathi Conversation - About hobby class

are kaushik, itakyA ghAIt kuThe jAtoyas?

I am also interested in learning guitar
मला पण गिटार शिकण्यात रस आहे.
malA paN giTAr shikaNyAt ras Ahe.
My class is 5 minutes from railway station
माझा क्लास रेल्वे स्टेशन पासून पाच मिनिटांवर आहे.
maajhA klAs relve sTeshan pAsUn pAch miniTAMvar aahe.
No. On alternate days. 5 to 6 in evening

nAhI. ekADaek divas asato. saMdhyAkALI pAch te sahA.

title: Simple Marathi Conversation-at-bus stop and in bus
content: Simple Marathi Conversation-at-bus stop and in bus
intro: Hinjewadi, Chinchwad, Dange Chowk, Phase-2 are places near Pune.

blocks.heading: Bus stop and in bus conversation
rows: I want to go to Hinjewadi. Where can I get bus?
मला हिंजवडीला जायचं आहे. बस कुठून मिळेल?
malA hiMjavaDIlA jAyachM Ahe. bas kuThUn miLel?
Does Hinjewadi bus start from here?
Uncle, do you know when is the next bus?
काका, तुम्हाला माहिती आहे का; पुढची बस कधी आहे ते?
kAkA, tumhAlA mAhitI Ahe kA? puDhachI bas kadhI Ahe te?
Oh god. It is half an hour more. Does any other bus go to Hinjewadi?
अरे बापरे. अजून अर्धा तास आहे. दुसरी कुठली बस जाते का हिंजवडीला.
are bApare. ajUn ardhA tAs Ahe. dusarI kuThalI bas jAte kA hiMjavaDIlA.
No. That is the only one. Catch bus for Chinchwad. Get down at Dange Chowk. Take another bus from there.
नाही ती एकच आहे. तुम्ही चिंचवडची बस पकडा. डांगे चौकात उतरा. आणि तिकडून दुसरी बस पकडा.
nAhI tI ekach Ahe. tumhI chiMchavaDachI bas pakaDA. DAMge chaukAt utarA ANi tikaDUn dusarI bas pakaDA.
Will I get bus for phase-3 directly?
Mostly you will get. Otherwise catch bus for phase-2
बहुतेक मिळेल. नाहितर फेज-२ पर्यंत जाणारी बस पकडा.
bahutek miLel. nAhitar phej-2 paryaMt jANArI bas pakaDA.
From phase-2 you will get share-auto for phase-3
फेज-२ पासून पुढे शेअर रिक्शा मिळते फेज-३ ला जायला.
phej-2 pAsUn puDhe shear rikshA miLate phej-3 lA jAyalA.
From where does bus for Chinchwad start?
From that next stop. Get in and stand in queue.
या पुढच्या स्टॉप पासून. आत रांगेत उभे रहा.
yA puDhachyA sT~op pAsUn. At rAMget ubhe rahA.
तिकिट बोला. / तुम्हाला कुठलं तिकिट पाहिजे
tikiT bolA. / tumhAlA kuThalaM tikiT pAhije
Please check carefully. There may be some in your bag.
I will give you 3 rupees afterwards. Take it while getting down
I do not know the stop. Can you tell me once we reach there?
मला स्टॉप माहित नाहिये. स्टॉप आला की सांगा.
malA sT~op mAhit nAhiye. sT~op AlA kI sAMgA.

pl~asTikachyA pishavyAMvar sadhyA baMdI Ahe. hi kAgadI pishavI ghyA.

title: Simple Marathi conversation : In Bank
content: Simple Marathi conversation : In Bank
intro: Video-chat session. Bank dialogue: account, DD, cheque, locker, forms.

blocks.heading: Bank conversation
rows: Hi, I want to open a saving account
नमस्कार, मला आपल्या बॅंकेत बचत खाते उघडायचे आहे.
namaskAr, malA ApalyA b~aMket bachat khAte ughaDAyache Ahe.
From where will I get form to open account?
खाते उघडण्यासाठी मला फॉर्म कुठून मिळेल?
khAte ughaDaNyAsAThI malA ph~orm kuThUn miLel?
I want to meet Branch manager
मला शाखा व्यवस्थापकांना भेटायचे आहे. ते कुठे बसतात?
malA shAkhA vyavasthApakAMnA bheTAyache Ahe. te kuThe basatAt?
I had applied for ATM card 1 month back. Not received yet
मी एक महिन्यापूर्वी एटीएम कार्डासाठी अर्ज केला होता पण अजून मिळाले नाही.
mI eka mahinyApUrvI eTIem kArDAsAThI arj kelA hotA paN ajUn miLAle nAhI.
How can I apply for cheque book?
I want to make a DD. Where is the form?
मला डिमांड ड्राफ्ट तयार करायचा आहे. डीडी बनवायसाठी मला फॉर्म कुठे मिळेल?
malA DimAMD DrAphT tayAr karAyachA Ahe. DIDI banavAyasAThI malA ph~orm kuThe miLel?
Please fill up this application form
कृपया खाते उघडण्यासाठी हा अर्ज भरा
kRupayA khAte ughaDaNyAsAThI hA arj bharA
How much do you want to deposit?
तुम्हाला तुमच्या खात्यात किती रक्कम जमा करायची आहे?
tumhAlA tumachyA khAtyAt kitI rakkam jamA karAyachI Ahe?
मी कमीतकमी कीती रक्कम जमा करू शकतो?
mI kamitakamI kItI rakkam jamA karU shakato?
How long for cheque to arrive?
मला चेक मिळायला किती वेळ लागेल?
mala chek milayala kiti vel lagel?
चेक येण्यासाठी अंदाजे दोन आठवडे लागतात.
chek yeNyAsAThI aMdAje don AThavaDe lAgatAt.
I want last year's account statements
मला मागच्या वर्षाचे अकाउंट स्टेटमेंट हवे आहे.
malA mAgachyA varShAche akAuMT sTeTameMT have Ahe.
मला माझ्या खात्यासाठी नेट बॅंकिगची सुविधा हवी आहे.
malA mAjhyA khAtyAsAThI neT b~aMkigachI suvidhA havI Ahe.
Attach two photos. Also AADHAR copy
याच्यावर दोन फोटो लावा. आणि आधार कार्डची कॉपी सुद्धा जोडावी लागेल.
yAchyAvar don phoTo lAvA. ANi AdhAr kArDachI k~opI suddhA joDAvI lAgel.
Why did you not write wife's name?
इथे पत्नीचे नाव का नाही लिहिले?
ithe patnIche nAv kA nAhI lihile?
साहेब माझे लग्न झाले नाही आहे.
sAheb mAjhe lagn jhAle nAhI Ahe.
Please take a photo and bring it

title: Simple Marathi Conversation - Informal phone conversation
content: Simple Marathi Conversation - Informal phone conversation
intro: Simple informal conversation on phone. Two scenarios: Joshi house call, and wrong number.

blocks.heading: Informal phone conversation
rows: I wanted to speak with Vaibhav Joshi
मला वैभव जोशी यांच्याशी बोलायचं होतं
malA vaibhav joshI yAMchyAshI bolAyachaM hotaM
Did you have any work with him?
Yes, wanted to give him a message
हो त्यांना एक निरोप द्यायचा होता
ho tyAMnA ek nirop dyAyachA hotA
Tell me. I will tell him once he is back
मला सांगा. मी त्यांना सांगतो आल्यावर.
malA sAMgA. mI tyAMnA sAMgato AlyAvar.
Ok. Tell him club meeting tomorrow at five
ठीक आहे. त्यांना सांगा उद्या क्लबची मीटिंग आहे. संध्याकाळी पाच वाजता.
ThIk Ahe. tyAMnA sAMgA udyA klabachI mITiMg Ahe. saMdhyAkALI pAch vAjatA.
Just wait. Door bell is ringing. I will open door
एक मिनिट थांबा दाराची बेल वाजत्ये. मी दार उघडतो.
ek miniT thAMbA dArAchI bel vAjatye. mI dAr ughaDato.
Hey Mr. Kulakarni, how are you?
I am fine. How are you?
I am fine too. Tell me. What were you up to?
मी पण मजेत. बोला काय काम होतं.
mI paN majet. bolA kAy kAm hotM.
Nothing special. Wanted to tell about meeting
विशेष काही नाही. मीटिंग बद्दल सांगायचं होतं.
visheSh kAhI nAhI. mITiMg baddal sAMgAyachaM hotaM.
I came to know. Mr. Rajwade met me. He told
मला कळलं त्याबद्दल. राजवाडे भेटले होते मला. ते बोलले.
malA kaLalM tyAbaddal. rAjavADe bheTale hote malA. te bolale.
I am Kaushik. Can I speak to Vaibhav?
मी कौशिक बोलतोय. वैभव आहेत का?
mI kaushik bolatoy. vaibhav Ahet kA?
No. This not number of Joshis

title: Simple Marathi Conversation - Interview in Marathi
content: Simple Marathi Conversation - Interview in Marathi
intro: Job interview dialogue. Resume, experience, family, hobbies, strengths.

blocks.heading: Interview conversation
rows: Tell me a bit about yourself
Primary education took place in Delhi
My college was also in Delhi
From where did you do M.B.A.?
I did MBA from IIM Ahmedabad
मी एम.बी.ए आय.आय.एम. अहमदाबाद मधून केलं.
mI em.bI.e Ay.Ay.em. ahamadAbAd madhUn kelM.
How much total experience do you have?
I have 10 years of experience in IT
माझा आय.टी. मधला अनुभव १० वर्षांचा आहे.
mAjhA Ay.TI. madhalA anubhav 10 varShAMchA Ahe.
Which companies have you worked in?
कुठल्या कंपन्यांमध्ये तुम्ही काम केलं आहे?
kuThalyA kaMpanyAMmadhye tumhI kAm kelM Ahe.
ABC, PQR and XYZ. Currently in XYZ company
ABC, PQR आणि XYZ. सध्या XYZ कंपनीत आहे.
ABC, PQR ANi XYZ. sadhyA kaMpanIt Ahe.
What kind of work do you do currently?
Which technologies do you work on?
Who all do you have at home?
Mother, Father, two brothers and one sister
आई, वडील, दोन भाऊ आणि एक बहीण.
AI, vaDIl, don bhAU ANi ek bahIN.
Yes. We all live in Pune
Elder brother is in bank. Other siblings are studying
मोठा भाऊ बॅंकेत आहे. बाकीची भावंडे शिकत आहेत.
moThA bhAU b~aMket Ahe. bAkIchI bhAvaMDe shikat Ahet.
What are your strengths and weaknesses?
तुमच्या स्ट्रेंथ्स आणि वीकनेस काय अहेत?
tumachyA sTreMths ANi vIkanes kAy ahet?
What do you expect from this job?
तुम्हाला या जॉब कडून काय अपेक्षा आहेत?
tumhAlA yA j~ob kaDUn kAy apekShA Ahet?
It was nice talking with you
Our H.R. will let you know

title: Simple Marathi conversation: Linking AADHAR card to mobile number
content: Simple Marathi conversation: Linking AADHAR card to mobile number
intro: Contribution by Saba. Linking Aadhaar to postpaid connection.

blocks.heading: AADHAR linking conversation
rows: Hello sir, how may I help you?
नमस्कार सर, मी तुम्हाला कशी मदत करु शकतो?
namaskar sar mi tumhala kashi madat karu shakato?
I received a message to link my Aadhaar with postpaid. Please tell me the procedure.
मला माझ्या पोस्टपेड कनेक्शनला आधार नंबर लिंक करण्याबद्दल मेसेज आला आहे. कृपया मला प्रक्रिया सांगा.
mala majhya postaped kanekshanala Adhar nambar link karanyabaddal mesej ala ahe. krupaya mala prakriya sanga.
Aadhar card alone is sufficient. Biometric test will validate.
तुमच्या मोबाईल नंबरच्या शिवाय फक्त आधार कार्ड पुरेसे आहे. पण दिलेला आधार क्रमांक प्रमाणित करण्यासाठी बायोमेट्रिक चाचणी घेतली जाईल
tumachya mobail nmbarachya shivay phakt Adhar kard purese ahe. pan dilela Adhar kramank pramanit karanyasathi bayometrik chachani ghetali jail.
Is the process chargeable? How many days?
काही चार्ज लागतो का? आणि सगळ्या गोष्टी सत्यापित करण्यासाठी किती दिवस लागतील?
kahi charj lagato ka? ani sagalya goshti satyapit karanyasathi kiti divas lagatil?
No it's free. Connection verified within a day.
हे विनामूल्य आहे आणि कनेक्शन एका दिवसात सत्यापित होऊन जाईल.
he vinamuly ahe ani kanekshan eka divasat satyapit houn jail.
You'll have to complete process for both numbers separately
तर तुम्हाला दोन्ही नंबरसाठी प्रक्रिया वेगवेगळी पूर्ण करावी लागेल.
tar tumhala donhi nmbarasathi prakriya vegavegali purn karavi lagel.
I have registered your both numbers. Check if you've received the OTP.
मी तुमच्या दोन्ही नंबरची नोंदणी करून दिली आहे. बघा OTP आला आहे का?
mi tumachya donhi nmbarachi nondani karun dili ahe. bagha OTP ala ahe ka?
You can go for finger print on that side now
हो झाले. आता तुम्ही तिकडे जाऊन फिंगर प्रिंट देऊ शकता.
ho jhale. ata tumhi tikade jaun phingar print deu shakata.
After Biometric verification, reply with 'Y' to confirmation message
बायोमेट्रिक सत्यापनानंतर तुम्हाला 24 तासांत पुष्टीकरण संदेश मिळेल. त्यावर 'Y' लिहून पाठवून द्या.
bayometrik satyapananantar tumhala 24 tasaat pushtikaran sandesh milel. tyavar 'Y' lihun pathavun dya.

title: Simple Marathi conversation: Linking mobile number to AADHAR card
content: Simple Marathi conversation: Linking mobile number to AADHAR card
intro: Contribution by Saba. Bala and Sakshi at Aadhaar center.

blocks.heading: Mobile to AADHAR conversation
rows: Bala - citizen who wants to link mobile number
बाला : आपला मोबाईल क्रमांक जोडण्यास इच्छुक असलेला नागरीक.
ApalA mobAIl kramAMk joDaNyAs ichChuk asalelA nAgarIk.
Sakshi - Help desk lady at Aadhaar center
साक्षी : आधार सेंटर येथे हेल्पडेस्क वर बसलेली महिला
sAkShI : AdhAr seMTar yethe helpaDesk var basalelI mahilA
Hello, how can I help you?
नमस्कार. मी काय मदत करू शकते तुमची.
namaskAr. mI kAy madat karU shakate tumachI.
I had received a message to link phone with Aadhaar
माझ्या मोबाईल नंबरवर, आधार क्रमांकाला फोन नंबर जोडण्याविषयी मला एक संदेश मिळाला होता.
mAjhyA mobAIl naMbaravar, AdhAr kramAMkAlA phon naMbar joDaNyAviShayI malA ek saMdesh miLAlA hotA.
Do I need to submit any request form?
बरं, त्यासाठी काही अर्ज द्यावा लागतो का?
barM, tyAsAThI kAhI arj dyAvA lAgato kA?
Same Aadhaar update form. Get from next photostat shop
होय, अ‍ॅक्च्युअली हा आधारपत्र वालाच फॉर्म भरायचा असतो. सध्या तुम्हाला पुढच्या फोटोस्टॅट शॉपमधून मिळून जाईल
hoy, ~akchyualI hA AdhArapatr vAlAch ph~orm bharAyachA asato. sadhyA tumhAlA puDhachyA phoTosT~aT sh~opamadhUn miLUn jAIl.
Will I need to resubmit ID proofs?
बरं. आणि सगळी कागदपत्रं परत सादर करायची गरज पडेल का?
barM. ANi sagaLI kAgadapatrM parat sAdar karAyachI garaj paDel kA?
One name, dob and address proof. Biometric tests redone.
हो. एक नाव, जन्मतारीख आणि पत्त्याचा पुरावा सादर करणे आवश्यक आहे.
ho. ek nAv, janmatArIkh ANi pattyAchA purAvA sAdar karaNe Avashyak Ahe.
नंबर लिंक होण्यासाठी किती दिवस लागतील?
naMbar liMk hoNyAsAThI kitI divasa lAgatIl?
सुमारे सात ते दहा दिवसांत होऊन जाईल. तुम्हाला तुमच्या नंबरवर मेसेज येईल.
sumAre sAt te dahA divasAMt hoUn jAIl. tumhAlA tumachyA naMbaravar mesej yeIl.

title: Simple Marathi Conversation - Marathi Language Proficiency Test (LPT) for banks
content:
Many of the learners of my tutorials are learning Marathi for competitive exams like bank, post, civil services etc. Recently Language Proficiency tests were conducted & many of the learners passed the exam well. I asked them to give more details so that others will get benefited by it.
Sbarna has shared her experience below.
Please find the details of my LPT :
We were given 3 topics to write a “Nibandh”(essay):(minimum 5 lines on any one topic)
1) Favourite festival
2) Favourite teacher
3) My family
I wrote 10 lines on my family.
Secondly, I was made to read a paragraph from a poem and asked to explain a line. Then the panel of 3 examiners conversed with me in Marathi. The questions were as follows:
Q)What is your name
? (
Tujha
naau kae ahe
?)
A) Maza
naav
Sabarna
ahe
.
Q)
tumhi kuthe rahates
?
A) Mi Airoli
madhe rahate
.
Q)
kiti varsha pasoon mumbaila rahate
?
A)Mi sade teen varsha pasoon mumbaila rahate
Q)Mumbai kashe ala?
A) Lagnanantar mi
mumbaila
ala.
Q) Marathi shikhniyasathi tumhi kae prayatna kela?
A)Mi Marathi shikhniyasathi Marathi Roz Marathi serial bagitli(konti serial?)
Mi Marathi movies bagitli (konti movies)
Mi Marathi shikhniyasathi tuition lawla ani toh Marathi bolnyachi, lihiniyachi ani vachnyachi abhyas kela.
Mi roz Marathi online newspapers vachnyachi abhyas kela.
mi majya Marathi mitrabarobar Marathi bolnyachi abhyas kela.
I have given the above conversation by rectifying small grammar and pronunciation mistakes. Also, I have given English translation.
What is your name ?
तुझं नाव काय आहे ?
tujhM nAv kAy Ahe ?
My name is Sabarna.
माझं नाव सबर्णा आहे. mAjhM naav sabarNA Ahe.
Where do you live ?
तू कुठे राहतेस?
tU kuThe rAhates?
I live in Airoli.
मी ऐरोली मध्ये राहते.
mI airolI madhye rAhate.
From how many years are you living in Mumbai ?
किती वर्षांपासून मुंबईला राहतेस?
kitI varShAMpAsUn muMbaIlA rAhates?
I am living in Mumbai from three and half years.
मी साडे तीन वर्षांपासून मुंबईला राहते.
mI sADe teen varShAMpAsUn muMbaIlA rAhate.
Why did you come to Mumbai ?
तू मुंबईला कशाला आलीस?
muMbaIlA kashAlA AlIs?
I came to Mumbai after marriage.
लग्नानंतर मी मुंबईला आले.
lagnAnaMtar mI muMbaIlA Ale.
How did you try to learn Maratih ?
मराठी शिकण्यासाठी तुम्ही काय प्रयत्न केला?
marAThI shikaNyAsAThI tumhI kAy prayatna kelA?
I watched Marathi serial everyday to learn Marathi.
I watched Marathi movies.
मी मराठी शिकण्यासाठी रोज मराठी सिरियल बघितली.
मी मराठी मूव्ही बघितल्या.
mI marAThI shikaNyAsAThI roj marAThI siriyal baghitalI.
mI marAThI mUvhI baghitalyA.
Which serial ? Which movies ?
कोणती सिरियल? कोणती मूव्ही ?
koNatI siriyal? koNatI mUvhI ?
XYZ, ABC
XYZ, ABC
I joined a tuition to learn Marathi & studied
Marathi speaking, writing and reading
मी मराठी शिकण्यासाठी ट्यूशन लावली
आणि मराठी बोलण्याचा, लिहिण्याचा आणि वाचण्याचा अभ्यास केला.
mI marAThI shikaNyAsAThI TyUshan lAvalI
ANi marAThI bolaNyAchA, lihiNyAchA ANi vAchaNyAchA abhyAs kelA.
I studied by reading online Marathi newspaper everyday
मी रोज मराठी ऑनलाईन न्यूजपेपर वाचण्याचा अभ्यास केला.
mI roj marAThI ~onalAIn nyUjapepar vAchaNyAchA abhyAs kelA.
I studied by talking in Marathi everyday
with my Marathi friend.
मी माझ्या मराठी मित्राबरोबर मराठी बोलण्याचा अभ्यास केला.
mI mAjhyA marAThI mitrAbarobar marAThI bolaNyAchA abhyAs kelA.

title: Simple Marathi Conversation - Marathi Language Proficiency Test (LPT) for banks
content: Simple Marathi Conversation - Marathi Language Proficiency Test (LPT) for banks
intro: Sbarna's LPT experience. Essay (Nibandh), poem reading, panel conversation. For bank, post, civil services exams.

blocks.heading: LPT conversation (rectified)
rows: From how many years in Mumbai?
I am living from three and half years
मी साडे तीन वर्षांपासून मुंबईला राहते.
mI sADe teen varShAMpAsUn muMbaIlA rAhate.
Why did you come to Mumbai?
I came to Mumbai after marriage
How did you try to learn Marathi?
मराठी शिकण्यासाठी तुम्ही काय प्रयत्न केला?
marAThI shikaNyAsAThI tumhI kAy prayatna kelA?
मी मराठी शिकण्यासाठी रोज मराठी सिरियल बघितली.
mI marAThI shikaNyAsAThI roj marAThI siriyal baghitalI.
मी मराठी शिकण्यासाठी ट्यूशन लावली आणि मराठी बोलण्याचा, लिहिण्याचा आणि वाचण्याचा अभ्यास केला.
mI marAThI shikaNyAsAThI TyUshan lAvalI ANi marAThI bolaNyAchA, lihiNyAchA ANi vAchaNyAchA abhyAs kelA.

title: Simple Marathi conversation - Quarrel at home
content: Simple Marathi conversation - Quarrel at home
intro: Sentences and phrases for household quarrel (भांडण / वादावादी) between family members.

blocks.heading: Quarrel conversation
rows: I am talking with you. Can you hear?
मी तुझ्याशी बोलतो आहे. तुला ऐकू येतंय का?
mI tujhyAshI bolato Ahe. tulA aikU yetMy kA
Why do you think I can't hear? I have not plugged my ears
ऐकू न यायला काय झालं? कानात बोळे नाही घातले मी
aikU n yAyalA kAy jhAlM? kAnAt boLe nAhI ghAtale mI
You are busy. Am I sitting idle?
तू बिझी आणि मी फुकट बसलो आहे का?
tU bijhI ANi mI phukaT basalo Ahe kA
I do all the work. But you do not value me
सगळी कामं मी करतो पण तुला माझी किंमतच नाहीये
sagaLI kAmM mI karato paN tulA mAjhI kiMmatach nAhIye
You are a fool. You don't have an iota of intelligence
तू मूर्ख/बावळट आहेस. तुला काडीची अक्कल नाही
tU mUrkh/bAvaLaT Ahes. tulA kADIchI akkal nAhI
Don't talk too much. Keep quiet
Did you do the task I told you yesterday?
I will do whenever its possible for me
Do it fast. It will be a great favour on me

title: Simple Marathi conversation : Republic Day 26th January celebration in India
content: Simple Marathi conversation : Republic Day 26th January celebration in India
intro: Sarah (Delhi) and Hana (South Korea) discuss Republic Day. Hana's dialogues highlighted. Thanks Saba SK.

blocks.heading: Context
blocks.content: Conversation between Sarah (भारत, दिल्ली) and Hana (दक्षिण कोरिया, स्योल).

blocks.heading: Republic Day conversation
rows: Hi Sara, I heard 26 Jan is holiday - Republic Day
हाना: हाय सारा. मला आत्ताच कळलं २६ जानेवारीला भारतात सुट्टी आहे कारण 26 जानेवारी हा इंडियाचा रिपब्लिक डे असतो!
hAnA hAy sArA. malA AttAch kaLalM jAnevArIlA bhAratAt suTTI Ahe kAraN jAnevArI hA iMDiyAchA ripablik De asato!
Yes, right. Prajasattak Din in Marathi
हो बरोबर. हो मराठीत प्रजासत्ताकदिन असं म्हणतात.
ho barobar. ho marAThIt prajAsattAkadin asM mhaNatAt.
आणि अखेरीस २६ जानेवारी १९५० रोजी संविधान अंमलात आले.
ANi akherIs jAnevArI rojI sMvidhAn aMmalAt Ale.
हा दिवस कसा साजरा केला जातो?
hA divas kasA sAjarA kelA jAto?
Flag hoisting and salute in morning
सकाळच्या वेळी ध्वज फडकवून, झेंडावंदन करून समारंभ सुरू केला जातो.
sakALachyA veLI dhvaj phaDakavUn, jheMDAvMdan karUn samArMbh surU kelA jAto.
Parade at Rajpath New Delhi, 3 days
राजपथ नवी दिल्ली येथे संरक्षण मंत्रायल एक कार्यक्रम आयोजित करते, जो तीन दिवस चालतो.
rAjapath navI dillI yethe sMrakShaN mMtrAyal ek kAryakram Ayojit karate, jo tIn divas chAlato.

title: Simple Marathi conversation - Rickshaw/Taxi driver
content: Simple Marathi conversation - Rickshaw/Taxi driver
intro: Conversation with rickshaw/taxi driver. Fare, meter, share auto, directions.

blocks.heading: Rickshaw conversation
rows: Can you take me to Tilak road?
I want passenger to Kothrud, Baner
मला कोथरूड, बाणेरचे भाडे हवे आहे
malA kotharUD, bANerache bhADe have aahe
टिळक रोड ला नक्की कुठे जायचेय?
TiLak roD lA nakkI kuThe jAyachey?
We came so near, how so much?
इतक्या जवळ आलो, तरी इतके कसे?
itakyA javaL aalo, tarI itake kase?
I am telling as per meter only
Where will I get share auto to Swargate?
Can you take me to Swargate? By share?

title: Simple Marathi Conversation - Software Engineers
content: Simple Marathi Conversation - Software Engineers
intro: Two software engineers meet outside a software park. Uses short forms. English words kept as-is (technology, hike, fix, etc.).

blocks.heading: Software Engineers conversation
rows: Hey, how come you are here? You were in ABC company, right?
अरे तू इकडे कसा काय? तू ABC कंपनीत होतास ना?
are tU ikaDe kasA kAy? tU ABC kaMpanIt hotAs nA?
Yes. I left it 2 months back
हो. दोन महिन्यांपूर्वी मी ती कंपनी सोडली
ho. don mahinyAMpUrvI mI tI kaMpanI soDalI
Wow!! But why did you leave?
I was not getting good work there
तिकडे मला काम चांगलं मिळालं नव्हतं.
tikaDe malA kAm chAMgalM miLAlM navhatM.
Which technology did you work on?
Great!! How much hike did you get?
40%. There I had package of 4. Here I got 5.6
40%. There I had package of 4. Here I got 5.6
And how much is your total experience?
How many years were you there?
Had you stayed one more year, you would have got gratuity
तिकडे अजून एक वर्ष थांबला असतास तर gratuity मिळाली असती
tikaDe ajUn ek varSh thAMbalA asatAs tar gratuity miLAlI asatI
Yes. But I was bored. I could not see growth there
हो. पण कंटाळा आला होता. तिथे काही growth दिसत नव्हती.
ho. paN kaMTALA aalA hotA. tithe kAhI growth disat navhatI.
It is OK. Not much. Work will be Nine-to-six
ठीक आहे. इतकं जास्त नाही. Nine-to-six काम होतं
ThIk Ahe. itakaM jAst nAhI. Nine-to-six kAm hotM
How do you travel to office?
It is near. At Rahul Garden City. Highway touch place
जवळच. राहूल Garden City मध्ये. High-way ला लागूनच आहे.
javaLach. rAhUl garden city madhye. High way lA lAgUnach Ahe.
Are you looking for a house?
Yes, the search is going on
My friend booked in Nature Park. 2BHK
माझ्या मित्राने nature park मधे बूक केला. 2BHK
mAjhyA mitrAne nature park madhe book kelA. 2bhk
It is cheap then. How much is rate there?
स्वस्त आहे की. किती Rate आहे तिकडे.
swast Ahe kI. kitI rate aahe tikaDe.
I will also go and see it
I leave now. I have one meeting now.
चल मी निघतो. आता एक meeting आहे.
chal mI nighato. AtA ek meeting aahe
I have your number. I give you miss-call
माझ्या कडे तुझा number आहे. miss-call देतो.
mAjhyA kaDe tujhA number Ahe. miss-call deto.

title: Simple Marathi Conversation - Teacher & Students
content: Simple Marathi Conversation - Teacher & Students
intro: Classroom conversation. Sentences highlighted are of students.

blocks.heading: Teacher & Students conversation
rows: Yesterday we learnt half of lesson
काल आपण अर्धा धडा शिकलो होतो.
kAl ApaN ardhA dhaDA shikalo hoto.
Did you understand everything taught yesterday?
Did you do homework given yesterday?
But mostly it will be from 15th
But start preparing from now onwards only
What is the noise in that corner?
त्या कोपऱ्यात काय गडबड चालू आहे?
tya koparyAt kAy gaDabaD chAlU Ahe?
Teacher, I am not doing anything
Then why is noise coming from there?
This Raju is telling joke to Sanju
हा राजू, संजू ला जोक संगतोय?
hA rAjU saMjU lA jok saMgatoy?
Raju, Is it time to tell joke?
राजू, ही जोक संगायची वेळ आहे?
rAjU, hI jok saMgAyachI veL Ahe?
If I hear noise again, I will make you stand for whole period
परत आवाज आला तर पूर्ण तासभर उभे करीन.
parat AvAj AlA tar pUrN tAsabhar ubhe karIn.

title: Simple Marathi conversation - Tour guide in Singapore speaks with Marathi tourists
content: Simple Marathi conversation - Tour guide in Singapore speaks with Marathi tourists
intro: Fiona guides Kesari group. Fiona learned Marathi phrases.

blocks.heading: Singapore tour conversation
rows: I am Fiona, your local tour guide
मी फिओना, तुमची स्थानिक टूर गाईड.
mI phionA, tumachI sthAnik TUr gAID.
For next 4 days I will accompany you
पुढचे चार दिवस मी तुमच्या बरोबर असेन आणि सिंगापूर बघण्यासाठी तुम्हाला मार्गदर्शन करेन.
puDhache chAr divas mI tumachyA barobar asen ANi siMgApUr baghaNyAsAThI tumhAlA mArgadarshan karen.
आज आपण Universal Studios ला भेट देणार आहोत.
Aj ApaN Universal Studios lA bheT deNAr Ahot.
We will reach there in 40 minutes
इथून चाळीस मिनिटांत आपण तिथे पोचू.
ithUn chALIs miniTAMt ApaN tithe pochU.
Follow the Kesari flag after getting down
बसमधून उतरल्यावर केसरीच्या झेंड्याच्या मागोमाग जा.
basamadhUn utaralyAvar kesarIchyA jheMDyAchyA mAgomAg jA.
याच ठिकाणी साडे चार वाजता आपण भेटू.
yAch ThikANI sADe chAr vAjatA ApaN bheTU.

title: Simple Marathi conversation-Vegetable Market सब्जी मंडी में मराठी संभाषण
content: Simple Marathi conversation-Vegetable Market
intro: Conversation in vegetable market. See List of vegetable names in Marathi for vocabulary.

blocks.heading: Vegetable Market conversation
rows: Aunty, How much is lady finger?
Five Rupees for a quarter kilogram
What is cost of half k.g.?
Ten for quarter k.g.. Eighteen for half k.g.
Take ten rupees for half k.g.
Ok. Take fifteen for half k.g.
Take it home. It will be a tasty vegetable.
घरी नेऊन बघा. छान भाजी होईल.
gharI neUn baghA. ChAn bhAjI hoIl.
Those vegetables and lemons; all is twenty
त्या भाज्या आणि लिंबं; सगळं धरून वीस झाले.
tyA bhAjyA ANi liMbM; sagaLaM dharUn vIs jhAle.
Now charge me just ten please.
Its first sale of my day. So not saying no
बोहनी आहे माझी. द्या. नाही नाही म्हणत
bohanI Ahe mAjhI. dyA. nAhI nAhI mhaNat
Wait, I give you plastic bag
Its nice that you gave it.

title: Simple Marathi conversation-Vegetable Market
content:
## Common vegetable names in Marathi
Here are common vegetables with their Marathi names and transliteration:

Potato: बटाटा (baTATA)
Onion: कांदा (kAMdA)
Carrot: गाजर (gAjar)
Cabbage: कोबी (kobI)
Cauliflower: फुलकोबी (phulakobI)
Tomato: टोमॅटो (TomaTo)
Cucumber: काकडी (kAkaDI)
Brinjal/Eggplant: वांगी (vAMgI)
Pumpkin: भोपळा (bhopaL)
Radish: मुळा (muLA)
Garlic: लसूण (lasUN)
Ginger: आले (Ale)
Bottle Gourd: दूधी भोपळा (dUdhI bhopaL)
Bitter Gourd: कारले (kArale)
Bell Pepper: शिमला मिरची (shimalA mirachI)
Green Chili: हिरवी मिरची (hiravI mirachI)
Lady's Finger/Okra: भेंडी (bheMDI)
Ridge Gourd: दोडका (doDakA)
Sweet Potato: रताळे (ratALe)
Green Peas: वाटाणा (vATANa)

title: Simple Marathi conversation : with friend's parents at lunch table
content: Simple Marathi conversation : with friend's parents at lunch table
intro: Boy and friend's parents. Two versions discussed.

blocks.heading: Lunch table conversation
rows: You came after so many days
Aunty my exams were going on
हो काकू. माझी परीक्षा चालू होती म्हणून मी नाही आलो.
ho kAkU. mAjhI parIkShA chAlU hotI mhaNUn mI nAhI Alo.
We are going to eat lunch. You too come
आता आम्ही जेवायला बसतो आहोत. तू सुद्धा ये.
AtA AmhI jevAyalA basato Ahot. tU suddhA ye.
No aunty. I am not much hungry
नको काकू मला जास्त भूक नाहीये.
nako kAkU malA jAst bhUk nAhIye.
Aunty, this dal is too tasty
काकू, ही डाळ खूप चविष्ट आहे.
kAkU, hI DAL khUp chaviShT Ahe.
Please give me a bit more
What do you love to eat?
Aunty, I like all vegetables but ladyfinger is my favourite
काकू मला सगळ्या भाज्या आवडतात पण मला भेंडी सगळ्यात जास्त आवडते.
kAkU malA sagaLyA bhAjyA AvaDatAt paN malA bheMDI sagaLyAt jAst AvaDate.
What's the time of dinner at your home?
Dinner time is not fixed. Mostly 9 or 10 pm
जेवायची वेळ नक्की नाही. बहुतेक वेळा आठ किंवा नऊ वाजता जेवतो.
jevAyachI veL nakkI nAhI. bahutek veLA ATh kiMvA naU vAjatA jevato.
नको, माझे पोट भरले आहे. आता मी जास्त नाही खाऊ शकत.
nakko, mAjhe poT bharale Ahe. AtA mI jAst nAhI khAU shakat.

title: Simple Marathi conversation - with housemaid
content: Simple Marathi conversation - with housemaid
intro: Conversation with maid. काकू/मावशी = aunty. Lady's dialogues highlighted.

blocks.heading: Housemaid conversation
rows: Will you do work at our place?
Washing clothes and dishes and floor-cleaning
आम्ही ४ मोठी माणसं आहोत आणि २ लहान मुलं आहेत.
AmhI 4 moThI mANasaM Ahot ANi 2 lahAn mulaM Ahet.
बरं. धुण्याचे सातशे आणि भांड्यांचे सातशे होतील.
baraM. dhuNyAche sAtashe ANi bhAMDyAMche sAtashe hotIl.
700 for clothes and dishes seems a lot
पण धुण्याचे आणि भांड्यांचे सातशे म्हणजे खूपच वाटतात.
paN dhuNyAche ANi bhAMDyAMche sAtashe mhaNaje khUpach vATatAt.
600 for clothes, 600 for dishes and 200 for cleaning
सहाशे धुण्याचे; सहाशे भांड्यांचे आणि दोनशे लादी पुसायचे.
sahAshe dhuNyAche; sahAshe bhAMDyAMche ANi donashe lAdI pusAyache.
I will take 4 holidays every month
Do not take unplanned leaves without informing
पण न सांगता दांड्या मारू नका.
hM. paN na sAMgatA dAMDyA mArU nakA.
Start the work from today itself
Pots-n-Dishes are in basin. Take them to bathroom
भांडी बेसिन मध्ये ठेवली आहेत ती मोरीत घेऊन जा.
bhAMDI besin madhye ThevalI Ahet tI morIt gheUn jA.
Soap and scrubber is there in that corner
Aunty, you did not give me bonus for Diwali

title: Simple Marathi conversation-with Servant or Housemaid नौकरानी के साथ मराठी संभाषण
content:
Below is a conversation between a lady with her maid. Dialogues of lady are highlighted in yellow.
आईए मौसी(AIe mausI)
या मावशी (yA mAvashI)
क्या आप हमारे यहां काम करोगी ?
(kyA Ap hamAre yahAM kAm karogI ?)
आमच्याकडचं काम कराल का?
(AmachyAkaDachM kAm karAl kA? )
क्या क्या काम है ?
(kyA kyA kAm hai ?)
काय काय काम आहे? (kAy kAy kAm Ahe?)
कपडे धोना, बर्तन मांजना, पोछा.
(kapaDe dhonA, bartan mAMjanA, poChA.)
धुणं, भांडी आणि केरफरशी
(dhuNaM, bhAMDI ANi kerapharashI)
कितने लोगोंके कपडे और बर्तन ?
(kitane logoMke kapaDe aur bartan ?)
किती जणांचं धुणं-भांडी
(kitI jaNAMchaM dhuNM-bhAMDI)
हम चार बडे लोग और दो बच्चे हैं.
(ham chaar baDe log aur do bachce haiM.)
आम्ही ४ मोठी माणसं आहोत आणि २ लहान मुलं आहेत.
(AmhI 4 moThI mANasaM Ahot ANi 2 lahAn mulaM Ahet.)
ठीक है. कपडे धोनेके सात सौ और बर्तन मांजनेके सात सौ होंगे.
(ThIk hai. kapaDe dhoneke sAt sau aur bartan mAMjaneke sAt sau hoMge.)
बरं. धुण्याचे सातशे आणि भांड्यांचे सातशे होतील.
(baraM. dhuNyAche sAtashe ANi bhAMDyAMche
sAtashe hotIl.)
पोछा लगनेके दो सौ.
(poChA laganeke do sau.)
लाद्या पुसायचे दोनशे. (lAdyA pusAyache donashe.)
हे भगवान ! पोछा लगनेके दो सौ ठीक है.
(he bhagavAn ! poChA laganeke do sau ThIk hai.)
बापरे!!  केरफरशीचे दोनशे ठीक आहेत.
(bApare kerapharashIche donashe ThIk Ahet. )
लेकीन कपडे और बर्तन के सात सौ मतलब कुछ ज्यादा ही लगते है.
(lekIn kapaDe aur bartan ke sAt sau matalab kuCh jyAdA hI lagate hai.)
पण धुण्याचे आणि भांड्यांचे सातशे म्हणजे खूपच वाटतात.
(paN dhuNyAche ANi bhAMDyAMche sAtashe mhaNaje
khUpach vATatAt.)
नही चाची.(nahI chAchI.)
नाही हो काकू.  (nAhI ho kAkU. )
पाच सौ ले लीजिये न मौसी.(pAcha sau le lIjiye n mausI.)
पाचशे घ्या ना मावशी.  (pAchashe ghyA nA mAvashI. )
नही चाची. सब जगह उतनेही लेते है.(nahI chAchI. sab jagah utanehI lete hai.)
नाही हो काकू. सगळीकडे तेवढेच घेतो.
(nAhI ho kAkU. sagaLIkaDe tevaDhech gheto.)
हर व्यक्तीके देड सौ लेते है.(har vyaktIke deD sau lete hai.)
माणशी दीडशेच घेतो.  (mANashI dIDashech gheto. )
आपके यहां दो छोटे बच्चे है इसलिये कम बताया.
(Apake yahAM do ChoTe bachche hai isaliye kam bataayA.)
तुमच्याकडे दोन लहान मुलं आहेत
म्हणून कमी सांगितले.
(tumachyAkaDe don lahAn mulaM Ahet
mhaNUn kamI sAMgitale.)
नहीं. सात सौ नहीं दे सकते.(nahIM. sAta sau nahIM de sakate.)
नाही. सातशे नाही जमणार.  (nAhI. sAtashe nAhI jamaNAr. )
पाच सौ नही तो छे सौ ठीक है.(pAch sau nahI to Che sau ThIk hai.)
पाचशे नाही तर सहाशे ठीक आहेत.
(pAchashe nAhI tar sahAshe ThIk Ahet.)
छे सौ कपडोंके, छे सौ बर्तनके और दो सौ पोछा लगाने के.
(Che sau kapaDoMke, Che sau bartanake aur do sau poChA lagAne ke.)
सहाशे धुण्याचे; सहाशे भांड्यांचे आणि दोनशे लादी पुसायचे.
(sahAshe dhuNyAche; sahAshe bhAMDyAMche ANi donashe
lAdI pusAyache. )
ठीक है ?(ThIk hai ?)
ठीक आहे? (ThIk Ahe?)
हां. चलेगा.(hAM. chalegA.)
हो चालेल.  (ho chAlel. )
और छुट्टियोंका क्या ?(aur ChuTTiyoMkA kyA ?)
आणि सुट्ट्यांचं काय. (ANi suTTyAMchaM kAy.)
महिनेमें ४ छुट्टिया लूंगी.(mahinemeM 4 ChuTTiyA lUMgI.)
महिन्यातून ४ सुट्ट्या घेत जाईन.
(mahinyAtUn 4 suTTyA ghet jAIn.)
४ ज्यादा होती है.(4 jyAdA hotI hai.)
४ जास्त होतात हो.  (4 jAst hotAt ho. )
नहीं चाची घरका काम भी संभालना पडता है.
(nahI chAchI gharakA kAm bhI saMbhAlanA paDatA hai.)
नाही काकू घरचं काम पण बघायला लागतं.
(nAhI kAkU gharachM kAm paN baghAyalA lAgataM. )
कोई मिलने आता है; मेहमान आते है. (koI milane AtA hai; mehamAn Ate hai. )
आलंगेलं-पैपाव्हणं असतं.
(AlaMgelM-paipAvhaNM asataM.)
हं. लेकिन बिना बताये छुट्टी मत लीजिये.(hM. lekin binA bataaye ChuTTI mat lIjiye.)
हं. पण न सांगता दांड्या मारू नका.
(hM. paN na sAMgatA dAMDyA mArU nakA.)
नही चाची. मै कभी भी बिना बताये छुट्टी नही लेती.
(nahI chAchI. mai kabhI bhI binA bataaye ChuTTI nahI letI.)
नाही काकू. मी कधीच न सांगता दांड्या मारत नाही.
(nAhI kAkU. mI kadhIch na sAMgatA dAMDyA mArat nAhI. )
कोई दिक्कत होगी तो पहलेही बता दूंगी नही तो फोन करूंगी.
(koI dikkat hogI to pahalehI batA dUMgI nahI to phon karUMgI.)
अडचण असेल तर आधी सांगेन नाही तर
फोन करेन तुम्हाला.
(aDachaN asel tar AdhI sAMgen nAhI tar
phon karen tumhAlA.)
ठीक है.(ThIk hai.)
बरं. (baraM.)
आज से ही शुरू करना काम.(Aja se hI shurU karanA kAm.)
आजपासूनच सुरुवात करा कामाला
(AjapAsUnach suruvAt karA kAmAlA.)
चलेगा.(chalegA.)
चालेल. (chAlel.)
बर्तन बेसिन में रखें है उन्हे बाथरूम में ले जाईये.
(bartan besin meM rakheM hai unhe bAtharUm meM le jAIye.)
भांडी बेसिन मध्ये ठेवली आहेत ती
मोरीत/बाथरूम मध्ये घेऊन जा.
(bhAMDI besin madhye ThevalI Ahet tI
morIt/bAtharUm madhye gheUn jA.)
साबुन, स्क्रबर वहां उस कोने में है.(sAbun, skrabar vahAM us kone meM hai.)
साबण, घासणी तिकडे कोपऱ्यात आहे.
(sAbaN, ghAsaNI tikaDe koparxyAt Ahe.)
बर्तन मांजने के बाद रसोई/किचन के प्लेटफार्म पर रख दीजिये.
(bartan mAMjane ke bAd rasoI/kichan ke pleTaphArm par rakh dIjiye.)
भांडी घासून झाली की
स्वैपाकखोलीच्या/किचनच्या ओट्यावर ठेवा. (
bhAMDI ghAsUn jhAlI kI
svaipAkakholIchyA/kichanachyA oTyAvar ThevA.)
यह पोछा लेना जमीन साफ करने के लिये.
(yah poChA lenA jamIn sAph karane ke liye.)
लादी पुसायला ती बादली घ्या. फिनेलची बाटली खिडकीत आहे.
(lAdI pusAyalA tI bAdalI ghyA. phinelachI bATalI khiDakIt Ahe. )
जमीन साफ करने के लिये वह बाल्टी लीजिये. फिनेल की बोतल खिडकी में है.
(jamIn sAph karane ke liye vah bAlTI lIjiye. phinel kI botal khiDakI meM hai.)
हे पोतेरं/फडकं घ्या लादी पुसायला.
(he poteraM/phaDakaM ghyA lAdI pusAyalA. )
और बेड के नीचे से, सोफा के निचे से, सब कुर्सिओंके नीचे से अच्छे से साफ करना.
(aur beD ke nIche se, sophA ke niche se, sab kursioMke nIche se
acChe se sAph karanA.)
आणि दिवाणाखालून, सोफ्याखालून,
सगळ्या खुर्च्यांखालून नीट पुसा.
(ANi divANAkhAlUn, sophyAkhAlUn,
sagaLyA khurchyAMkhAlUn nIT pusA.)
हां जरूर. आप चिंता मत कीजिये. मै ठीक से साफ करती हूं.
(hAM jarUr. Ap chiMtA mat kIjiye. mai ThIk se sAph karatI hUM.)
हो नक्की. काही काळजी करू नका.
मी स्वच्छ पुसते.
(ho nakkI. kAhI kALajI karU nakA.
mI svachCha pusate.)
बर्तन मांजने के पहले कपडे भीगोकर रखना तकी अच्छे से साफ हो.
(bartan mAMjane ke pahale kapaDe bhIgokar rakhanA
takI achChe se sAph ho.)
भांडी घासायच्या आधी कपडे भिजवून ठेवा
म्हणजे नीट निघतील.
(bhAMDI ghAsAyachyA AdhI kapaDe bhijavUn ThevA
mhaNaje nIT nighatIl.)
ठीक है.(ThIk hai.)
ठीके. (ThIke.)
यह आपकी तनख्वा.(yah ApakI tanakhvA.)
हा घ्या तुमचा पगार. (hA ghyA tumachA pagAr.)
दीजिये.(dIjiye.)
द्या. (dyA.)
चाची. दिवाली का बोनस नही दिया.(chAchI. divAlI kA bonas nahI diyA.)
काकू दिवाळीचा बोनस नाही दिलात.
(kAkU divALIchA bonas nAhI dilAt.)
दिवाली का बोनस ?(divAlI kA bonas ?)
दिवाळीचा बोनस ? (divALIchA bonas ?)
हां चाची. सब लोग देते है दिवाली का बोनस. तनख्वा जितनी और तनख्वा.
(hAM chAchI. sab log dete hai divAlI kA bonas. tanakhvA jitanI aur tanakhvA.)
हो काकू. सगळे जण दिवाळीचा बोनस देतात.
पगारा एवढा पगार.
(ho kAkU. sagaLe jaN divALIchA bonas detAt.
pagArA evaDhA pagAr.)
नहीं मौसी. तनख्वा जितनी और तनख्वा नही हो सकेगा.
(nahIM mausI. tanakhvA jitanI aur tanakhvA nahI ho sakegA.)
नाही हो मावशी. पगारा एवढा पगार नही जमणार.
(nAhI ho mAvashI. pagArA evaDhA pagAr nahI jamaNAr.)
दिवाली त्योहार है इसलिये सौ-दो सौ ज्यादा देती हूं.
(divAlI tyohAr hai isaliye sau-do sau jyAdA detI hUM.)
दिवाळी सण म्हणून शंभर-दोनशे जास्त देते.
(divALI saN mhaNUn shMbhar-donashe jAst dete.)
हमने जब तनख्वा की बात की तब ऐसा कुछ तय नही किया था.
(hamane jab tanakhvA kI bAt kI tab aisA kuCh tay nahI kiyA thaa.)
आपलं पगाराचं बोलणं झालं तेव्हा असं काही ठरलं नव्हतं.
(ApalM pagArAchaM bolaNM jhAlM tevhA asaM
kAhI TharalaM navhataM.)
ऐसे कैसे चाची. सब लोग देते है.(aise kaise chAchI. sab log dete hai.)
असं कसं काकू. सगळीकडेच देतात.
(asaM kasaM kAkU. sagaLIkaDech detAt. )
मैने कुछ अलग नही कहा.(maine kuCh alag nahI kahA.)
म्हणून मी काही वेगळं संगितलं नाही.
(mhaNUn mI kAhI vegaLM saMgitalaM nAhI.)
ये लीजिये चार सौ ज्यादा देती हूं. इससे ज्यादा नही हो पायेगा.
(ye lIjiye chAr sau jyAdA detI hUM. isase jyAdA nahI ho pAyegA.  )
हे घ्या चारशे जास्ती देते. याहून जास्त नाही जमणार.
(he ghyA chaarashe jAstI dete. yaahUn jAst nAhI jamaNAr.)
अब मै क्या बोलूं ? दीजिये.(ab mai kyA bolUM ? dIjiye.)
आता काय बोलणार मी. द्या. (AtA kaay bolaNAr mI. dyaa.)
चलती हूं मै.(chalatI hUM mai.)
येते मी. (yete mI.)
